> **Ink cost (account-wide full bleed standard, established 2026-06-01):** $0.50/sq ft × 0.231 sq ft = $0.1155/label. Account-wide rule per `PRICING_RULES.md` §25.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both dimensions (7.00" and 4.75") fit within the laminator's 13.5" maximum width capacity independently, so lamination orientation is a **nesting-efficiency choice**, not a forced constraint (contrast with 3024595, where only the narrow dimension fit at all).

- 7.00" across laminator: 6.50" clearance — single pass ✓
- 4.75" across laminator: 8.75" clearance — single pass ✓

**One lamination pass**, consistent with every other single-label item on this account.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 7" × 4.75". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28". Putting the 7" dimension across the 28" print bed nests **4 labels per row with zero waste** (4 × 7" = 28.00", 0" clearance) — the more efficient of the two orientations (4.75" across the bed would nest only 5 per row at 23.75" used, 4.25" clearance, and does not divide the bed evenly). The 4.75" dimension runs in the feed direction for both print and laminate, matching the laminator's 8.75" clearance orientation above.

- Labels per row: 4 (7" × 4 = 28", zero waste)
- At qty 20: 5 rows × 4 labels = 20 labels. Zero-waste single-orientation run.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-07-14. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` / `materials/orajet-3951-white.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22, $1.21/sq ft) and `materials/1mil-polyester-overlaminate.md` (1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28, $0.2389/sq ft); full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01. **Computed fresh per the §25 canonical formula — price is locked, but the cost build below is derived directly from the account's verified material rates, per session instruction.**
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.231 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.2795 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.231 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.0552 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.231 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.1155 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.4502** |
| Incidental buffer (minimal, conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.0098 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.46** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste. Here $0.4502 rounds up to $0.46 — a minimal conservative round-up, the same filing convention used on P/N 3024595 ($0.9511 → $0.96).

---
sections.production:
1. File import/setup — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22). File prep is $0 per Core Rule 7.
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. Full bleed / full coverage ANSI danger label artwork. 4 labels per row at 7" across the print bed (zero waste).
3. Laminate — 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate. **ONE pass.** Both dimensions clear the 13.5" laminator independently, so orientation is a nesting-efficiency choice (4.75" runs across the laminator, matching the print-step feed direction), not a forced constraint.
4. Contour cut — Roland follows registration marks from the print step.
5. Weed — remove excess material.
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, ANSI color/format compliance.
7. Package — flat, stacked.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Estimated Production Time (batch of 20):** ~20–25 min total (5 min file import + 15–20 min print/lam/cut/weed/inspect/package) — consistent with other single-pass singles on this account.
- **Constraints:** None — both dimensions fit the laminator, print bed nests at zero waste. No forced orientation, no bottleneck.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (6 models × 4 waves), followed by a POST-SYNTHESIS §31 CORRECTION on the deep tiers. Final price locked by Nick prior to this session's file-write. Claude Code did not re-derive or second-guess this price — it is final.**

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 1001220 DANGER ELECTROCUTION HAZARD, UNINSULATED PLATFORM. 7" × 4.75" = 0.230903 sq ft (filed 0.231). Single label, 1 piece. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. ANSI danger label (§29 account rule). 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope routing (Decision Tree Step 1a from `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`):**

| Threshold | Value | Result |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| sq ft < 0.1? | 0.231 ≥ 0.1 | NOT Micro-Format Band |
| 0.1 ≤ sq ft < 0.5? | 0.231 in range | **SUB-SCOPE HANDLING** |
| sq ft ≥ 0.5? | 0.231 < 0.5 | NOT Singles Band |

**Routing: Sub-scope (0.1–0.5 sq ft).** Smaller than both 3024595 (0.488 sq ft — the item that established the §31 doctrine) and 1210810 (0.292 sq ft — pre-§31 grandfathered). This is NOT a singles-band item.

---

## Step 2 — Governing Comparables

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft (qty 20) | Notes |
|------|-------|-----------------|-------------------|-------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark / hard floor) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root — hard floor, applies at every tier per §31 |
| 3024595 (largest sub-scope, §31-founding) | 0.488 | $7.75 | $15.88 | Flat 20-49 through 200+ — establishes the §31 doctrine this item inherits |
| 3017572 | 0.365 | $6.00 | $16.44 | §31-compliant 1-9 through 50-99; One-Time Exception below floor on 100-199/200+ |
| 1210810 (nearest comparable below on gradient) | 0.292 | $4.75 | $16.27 | Pre-§31 grandfathered |
| **1001220 (this item)** | **0.231** | **$4.00** | **$17.32** | **Sits between 1279130 and 1210810 on the sub-scope gradient** |
| 1279130 (nearest comparable above on gradient) | 0.148 | $3.10 | $20.95 | Sub-scope |
| 1101250 | 0.132 | $2.25 | $17.05 | Documented gradient exception — do not benchmark against |
| 3020477 | 0.130 | $2.75 | $21.15 | Sub-scope |
| 1247120 | 0.122 | $2.75 | $22.54 | Sub-scope |

1001220 sits correctly between 1279130 ($20.95/sq ft at 0.148 sq ft) and 1210810 ($16.27/sq ft at 0.292 sq ft) — consistent with the sub-scope premium doctrine that smaller labels carry higher $/sq ft than larger ones on this gradient.

---

## Step 3 — Material Cost (Computed Fresh This Session)

Material cost below is computed from the actual verified material files in this repo (`materials/orajet-3951-white.md`, `materials/1mil-polyester-overlaminate.md`, and the §25 canonical ink rate).

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.231 × $1.21 | $0.2795 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.231 × $0.2389 | $0.0552 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 0.231 × $0.50 | $0.1155 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.4502** |
| Incidental buffer (minimal, account convention) | judgment-applied | + $0.0098 |
| **Canonical material cost** | | **$0.46** |

**$/sq ft at qty 20:** $4.00 / 0.231 = **$17.32/sq ft** — above the $15.43/sq ft root floor at every tier (see Step 6).

---

## Step 4 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary (Already Complete — Recorded, Not Re-Derived)

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation, 6 top-tier models per wave, run in Claude Chat per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. Nick is sole decision-maker; the responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build):** No convergence — qty-20 prices ranged $3.25–$5.50 across the 6 models.

**Wave 2 (Destruction):** The median-based ladder from Wave 1 was attacked; 4 of 6 verdicts "No." Dominant finding: the small-format premium over the P/N 1230820 root benchmark **widened with volume instead of shrinking** (~19% at qty 20 → ~53% at 200+) — backwards from genuine fixed-cost economics, where the premium should compress, not expand, as volume grows. The ladder was revised: low/mid tiers (1-9 through 50-99) converged; deep tiers (100-199, 200+) split into two competing paths — **flat-hold (~$3.75)** vs. **step-down (~$3.00/$2.75)**.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation):** All 6 models unanimously preferred the step-down path, reasoning that a flat-hold structure would read to Sean Finn as "no volume curve exists" and trigger pushback at the 100-199 tier.

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis):** 5 of 6 models returned "No" on the step-down ladder, unanimously converging on a separate defect — a premium "wobble" at the 50-99 tier — and recommending a corrected step-down ladder: **$4.00 / $3.50 / $3.00 / $2.75** at 20-49 through 200+.

**Nick's decision:** Before locking the Wave 4 step-down ladder, checked it against `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 — see Step 5.

---

## Step 5 — Post-Synthesis §31 Correction

`governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 (sub-scope root floor applies at every tier) was established 2026-07-01 via P/N 3024595 — **after** `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`'s last update. It was therefore never embedded in any of this item's four wave prompts, and none of the 24 model responses could have flagged a §31 violation.

Checking the Wave 4 step-down ladder against §31:

| Tier | Wave 4 Recommendation | $/Sq Ft | §31 Floor ($15.43/sq ft)? |
|------|------------------------|---------|------------------------------|
| 50-99 | $3.50 | $15.15 | ✗ **Violation — $0.28/sq ft below floor** |
| 100-199 | $3.00 | $12.99 | ✗ **Violation — $2.44/sq ft below floor** |
| 200+ | $2.75 | $11.90 | ✗ **Violation — $3.53/sq ft below floor** |

All three deep tiers of the Wave 4 step-down ladder price below the $15.43/sq ft hard floor — a direct §31 violation.

**Correction applied per §31:** Held flat at **$3.75/ea ($16.23/sq ft)** — the cheapest $0.25-increment price that clears the floor — from the 50-99 tier through 200+. This mirrors P/N 3024595's own flat-ladder structure exactly, consistent with the doctrine that once a sub-scope item's economics reach the floor, a flat tier structure (not a further step-down) is the correct and only $0.25-compliant (§30) outcome.

**Nick locked this corrected ladder** — $7.00 / $5.50 / $4.00 / $3.75 / $3.75 / $3.75. Claude Code did not re-derive or re-litigate this price; the correction was a §31 compliance check applied before locking, not a re-derivation of the validated 1-9 through 20-49 tiers (which are unmodified engine consensus).

---

## Step 6 — Floor Doctrine Verification (Final, Locked Tiers)

The $15.43/sq ft root floor (P/N 1230820) is verified at every tier on this item's final, locked tier table:

| Tier | Price | $/Sq Ft | Above $15.43/sq ft Floor? |
|------|-------|---------|---------------------------|
| 1-9 | $7.00 | $30.30 | ✓ Yes (+96.4%) |
| 10-19 | $5.50 | $23.81 | ✓ Yes (+54.3%) |
| 20-49 | $4.00 | $17.32 | ✓ Yes (+12.3%) |
| 50-99 | $3.75 | $16.23 | ✓ Yes (+5.2%) |
| 100-199 | $3.75 | $16.23 | ✓ Yes (+5.2%) |
| 200+ | $3.75 | $16.23 | ✓ Yes (+5.2%) |

**No tier on this item breaches the floor.** This is the first sub-scope item to reach a fully §31-compliant flat structure directly (i.e., without a Nick-directed One-Time Exception override below the floor, unlike 3017572 and 1101250).

---

## Step 7 — §26 Invoice Protection Check

Unlike 3024595 (flat from 20-49 through 200+), this item's 20-49 tier ($4.00) differs from its flat 50-99+ tiers ($3.75), so three tier boundaries carry a per-unit price change and require verification:

| Boundary | Smaller Qty Total | Larger Qty Total | Cliff | Resolution |
|----------|--------------------|--------------------|-------|-----------|
| 9/10 | 9 × $7.00 = $63.00 | 10 × $5.50 = $55.00 | $8.00 | §26: buyer charged $55.00 for qty 9 |
| 19/20 | 19 × $5.50 = $104.50 | 20 × $4.00 = $80.00 | $24.50 | §26: buyer charged $80.00 for qty 19 |
| 49/50 | 49 × $4.00 = $196.00 | 50 × $3.75 = $187.50 | $8.50 | §26: buyer charged $187.50 for qty 49 |

The 99/100 and 199/200 boundaries are flat by definition ($3.75 = $3.75) and carry no cliff risk. All three active boundaries resolve cleanly under §26 — no inversions. No additional quote-email language required beyond the standard §26 handling already applied account-wide.

---

sections.notes:
> ## §31 APPLIED VIA POST-SYNTHESIS CORRECTION — NOT AN ENGINE-NATIVE FINDING
>
> This item's 4-wave validation ran without `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 embedded in the wave prompts, because §31 (established 2026-07-01 via P/N 3024595) postdates the last update to `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. All four waves — including the Wave 4 Final Synthesis recommendation of a step-down ladder ($3.50/$3.00/$2.75 at 50-99/100-199/200+) — priced three deep tiers below the $15.43/sq ft hard floor. This was caught and corrected **after** Wave 4, before Nick locked the final table: the 50-99 through 200+ tiers were held flat at $3.75/ea ($16.23/sq ft), mirroring 3024595's structure. **Process finding, not actioned this session:** `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` Section 3 should be updated in a future session to embed §31 in the band-context block so future sub-scope wave prompts don't repeat this gap. This item and P/N 1101250 (Session AM) are now two consecutive sub-scope items whose validation predated a governance rule that had to be applied post-hoc — a pattern worth closing.

---

> ## SIZE-COMPARISON NOTE — EDITORIAL CORRECTION, NO PRICING IMPACT
>
> The session prompt's narrative framing described this item as "larger than 1210810 (0.292 sq ft)." At 0.231 sq ft, 1001220 is in fact **smaller** than 1210810 (0.292 sq ft) — it sits between 1279130 (0.148 sq ft) and 1210810 (0.292 sq ft) on the sub-scope size gradient. This file corrects that size-relationship description for accuracy; the locked tier table, material cost, and all pricing figures are unchanged and were not re-derived. Also note: this is the account's **eighth** sub-scope data point counting all sub-scope items on file (1210810, 1247120, 1279130, 3020477, 1101250, 3017572, 3024595, and now 1001220) — the "third" framing in the session prompt refers specifically to the root-benchmark/§31 lineage (1210810 pre-§31 → 3024595 founding §31 → 1001220 first direct §31 inheritance), not the total sub-scope count.

---

>
> Unlike 3024595, where only one orientation fit the laminator, both dimensions of 1001220 (7.00" and 4.75") independently fit the laminator's 13.5" width capacity. Per `PRICING_RULES.md` §29, all items printed on Orajet 3951 with polyester laminate on this account are ANSI by default — no separate ANSI premium or discount is applied; the sub-scope gradient pricing already reflects the account's full-bleed and complexity assumptions.

5. Weed — remove outer excess material.
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, color fidelity.
7. Package — flat, stacked.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1

- **Constraints:** None. All dimensions well within equipment limits. No special orientation required for lamination.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-06-22 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves). Wave 4 6/6 unanimous YES. Final price locked by Nick.**
>
> This item is the **first controller/panel label on the Elliott account** and the **first Micro-Format Band item parity-governed from the non-ANSI control label family**. The qty-20 price is set by parity with P/N 3024140, not by the linear $/sq ft formula or the sub-0.06 sq ft per-label floor.

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 1012080 LABEL, PTE SINGLE STICK CONTROLLER. 2.8125" × 3.9375" = 0.076904 sq ft (filed 0.077). Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. Non-ANSI equipment controller/panel label. 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope:** 0.077 sq ft is below 0.1 sq ft — routes to the Micro-Format Band for production catalog items. This item is a **production catalog item** (not a one-off / field-service request), so the Micro-Format Band framework governs, not the $55 tiny one-off floor.

**Sub-band position:** 0.077 sq ft is ABOVE the ~0.06 sq ft per-label floor threshold. The floor applies below ~0.06 sq ft only. At 0.077 sq ft:

| Method | Result at qty 20 |
|--------|-----------------|
| Linear Micro-Format Band ($30.86/sq ft × 0.077 sq ft) | $2.38 |
| Per-label floor — complexity-dependent (sub-0.06 sq ft only) | **Does NOT apply at 0.077 sq ft** |
| Parity with 3024140 (non-ANSI control label family) | **$2.50 — GOVERNS** |

The linear formula at $2.38 is technically applicable at 0.077 sq ft (above the floor threshold), but parity with the existing non-ANSI control label family at $2.50 is the governing logic. The $0.12 delta above the linear formula is the parity premium — intentional and 4-wave validated.

---

## Step 2 — Benchmark and Parity Analysis

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft | Complexity Class | Method |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|-----------------|--------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | N/A | Singles band root |
| 1279000 (Micro-Format Band founding) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | ANSI Z535.4 WARNING | Micro-Format Band anchor |
| 1278220 (Parity clone of 1279000) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | ANSI Z535.4 WARNING | Inherited from 1279000 |
| 3024592 (sub-0.06 sq ft ANSI floor) | 0.054 | $2.75 | $50.93 | ANSI Z535.6 DANGER | Per-label floor (ANSI) |
| 3024140 (non-ANSI control label) | 0.019 | $2.50 | $131.58 | Non-ANSI — simple control label | Per-label floor (non-ANSI) |
| **1012080 (this item)** | **0.077** | **$2.50** | **$32.47** | **Non-ANSI — controller/panel label** | **Parity-governed from 3024140 at qty 20** |

**Parity logic:** 1012080 at $2.50/qty 20 = $32.47/sq ft, intentionally above the $30.86/sq ft linear formula output. The $/sq ft of $32.47 is a natural artifact of the parity pricing — it is NOT the pricing basis. The governing number is **$2.50 per label at qty 20 by parity with P/N 3024140**. The $0.12 above the linear formula reflects the non-ANSI control/panel label family floor: Sean's existing P/N 3024140 already establishes $2.50 as the floor for simple non-ANSI control labels on this account, and P/N 1012080 is the same classification. Setting 1012080 below $2.50 (at $2.38 per the linear formula) would create a pricing inconsistency within the non-ANSI control label family that Sean could immediately identify.

**Band anchor: unchanged.** This item's parity pricing does NOT alter the Micro-Format Band anchor of $30.86/sq ft at qty 20 (1279000). 1012080 is a parity-governed data point within the band, not a new independent band data point. The band anchor remains 1279000.

---

## Step 3 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 total independent responses. Nick is sole decision-maker.

**Wave 1 (Build Round):** All 6 models built pricing from specs, material cost ($0.16), and benchmark data. Primary anchor: P/N 3024140 ($2.50 at qty 20, non-ANSI control label family). Models recognized this item as a non-ANSI controller/panel label at 0.077 sq ft — above the sub-0.06 floor threshold, within the Micro-Format Band. 5/6 proposed $2.50 at qty 20 via parity with 3024140; 1/6 proposed $2.38 per the linear formula. Central tendency: $2.50. Initial 200+ tier at $1.75.

**Wave 2 (Destruction Round):** Primary structural finding: the initial 200+ tier at $1.75 sat ~30% below the $2.50 qty-20 anchor. At $1.75/$2.50 = 70% of anchor, the 200+ tier would anchor the entire controller/panel label catalog family below floor — below even 3024140's $1.75 in relative position, but creating an anchor that future controller/panel label items in the 200+ tier would reference downward. 6/6 models flagged 200+ as the weakest tier; 5/6 recommended raising to $1.90; 1/6 to $2.00. Buyer attack: $2.50 at qty 20 passes Sean's existing $2.50/3024140 mental model without friction — identical rate, new controller label. No questions expected. Competitor attack: $2.50 is consistent with the $2.50–$3.25 range confirmed for comparable items. Cost auditor: material at $0.16 is 6.4% of price — fixed labor dominates; no cost-plus risk.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation as Sean Finn):** 6/6 simulations approved at $2.50 for qty 20, zero pushback. Sean's mental model: he already has 3024140 at $2.50; 1012080 at the same rate is fully consistent. Pushback threshold $2.75–$3.00. Quote email anchor confirmed: *"Priced consistent with your existing P/N 3024140 non-ANSI control label — full bleed, no file prep, no MOQ."* 200+ at $1.90 passed in all 6 simulations — visible step-down from $2.50, reasonable for high volume.

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis):** Cold atomic sessions with full dossier including Wave 2 structural finding (200+ raised from $1.75 to $1.90).
- **6/6 unanimous YES — send as shown with 200+ at $1.90.**
- All 6 confirmed $2.50 at qty 20 as the correct parity anchor with 3024140.
- All 6 confirmed 200+ at $1.90 is the correct floor (not $1.75 — the structural anchor risk is real and documented).
- No model requested any modification.

**Nick's decision:** Locked $2.50 at qty 20. Engine consensus accepted; no override. 200+ set at $1.90 per Wave 2/4 structural finding. Full tier table: $3.75 / $2.90 / $2.50 / $2.20 / $1.95 / $1.90.

---

## Step 4 — Tier Structure and Parity Documentation

**Tier ratios from 1279000 Micro-Format Band template (reference only — parity governs at qty 20):**
- 1-9: 1.50× anchor ($2.50 × 1.50 = $3.75) ✓ — tier ratio consistent with Micro-Format Band template
- 10-19: 1.16× anchor ($2.50 × 1.16 = $2.90) ✓
- 20-49: 1.00× anchor ($2.50) — PARITY ANCHOR
- 50-99: 0.88× anchor ($2.50 × 0.88 = $2.20) ✓
- 100-199: 0.78× anchor ($2.50 × 0.78 = $1.95) ✓
- 200+: 0.76× anchor ($2.50 × 0.76 = $1.90) ✓ (raised from initial $1.75 per Wave 2 finding)

Note: the 200+ at $1.90 = 76% of anchor. 3024140's 200+ at $1.75 = 70% of anchor. 1012080's slightly higher 200+ floor is intentional — it protects the controller/panel label catalog family anchor without compromising the complexity-differentiation between 1012080 ($1.90) and 3024140 ($1.75). The $0.15 step-down at 200+ preserves the family structure.

**Relationship to existing Micro-Format Band items:**
- 1279000 (0.097 sq ft): $3.00/qty 20 = $30.86/sq ft — ANSI WARNING, band anchor
- 1278220 (0.097 sq ft): $3.00/qty 20 = $30.86/sq ft — ANSI WARNING, parity clone of 1279000
- **1012080 (0.077 sq ft): $2.50/qty 20 = $32.47/sq ft — non-ANSI controller label, PARITY-GOVERNED from 3024140**
- 3024592 (0.054 sq ft): $2.75/qty 20 — ANSI DANGER, per-label floor governs
- 3024140 (0.019 sq ft): $2.50/qty 20 — non-ANSI control label, per-label floor governs, PARITY SOURCE

1012080 prices at parity with 3024140 ($2.50) despite being much larger (0.077 vs 0.019 sq ft). This is correct: at this size class, the per-label economics are dominated by fixed production labor, not material area. The parity reflects that both items are non-ANSI control labels of similar production complexity.

---

## §26 Invoice Protection — Tier Boundary Verification

| 19/20 | 19 × $2.90 = $55.10 | 20 × $2.50 = $50.00 | $5.10 | §26 resolves — buyer charged $50.00 for qty 19 |
| 49/50 | 49 × $2.50 = $122.50 | 50 × $2.20 = $110.00 | $12.50 | §26 resolves |
| 99/100 | 99 × $2.20 = $217.80 | 100 × $1.95 = $195.00 | $22.80 | §26 resolves |
| 199/200 | 199 × $1.95 = $388.05 | 200 × $1.90 = $380.00 | $8.05 | §26 resolves |

All cliffs resolved automatically at billing time by §26 invoice protection — no quote-email language required.

>

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 7.25" width and 10.00" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Single lamination pass in either orientation. No laminator complications.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 7.25" × 10.00". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

- **Orientation A** (7.25" across feed, 10.00" in feed direction): 7.25" across the 28" print bed → up to 3 labels across (3 × 7.25" = 21.75" ≤ 28").
- **Orientation B** (10.00" across feed, 7.25" in feed direction): 10.00" across the 28" bed → up to 2 labels across (2 × 10.00" = 20" ≤ 28").

Nesting is identical to P/N 1068270 and P/N 1073950 — Orientation A (3-across) is the optimal layout at higher quantities; both orientations fit cleanly at low quantities.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 7.25" width and 10.00" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Single lamination pass in either orientation. No laminator complications.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 7.25" × 10.00". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

- **Orientation A** (7.25" across feed, 10.00" in feed direction): 7.25" across the 28" print bed → up to 3 labels across (3 × 7.25" = 21.75" ≤ 28").
- **Orientation B** (10.00" across feed, 7.25" in feed direction): 10.00" across the 28" bed → up to 2 labels across (2 × 10.00" = 20" ≤ 28").

Nesting is identical to 1082570 — orientation A (3-across) is the optimal layout at higher quantities; both orientations fit cleanly at low quantities.

|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.503 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.6086 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.503 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.1202 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed per §25) | 0.503 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.2515 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.9803** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) ¹ | judgment-applied | − $0.0003 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.98** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per `PRICING_RULES.md` §25):** The calculated material total is filed to the nearest cent per account convention — here $0.9803 files at $0.98, $0.0003 below the strict raw calculation and well within the documented §25 compliance tolerance used across this category (compare 1267140: calculated $1.0914 filed $1.09, −$0.0014; 3020477: calculated $0.2534 filed $0.25, −$0.0034).

**Filing-convention note:** P/N 1068270 (identical 0.503 sq ft area, same material system) files its material cost at $1.00 — a slightly larger buffer reflecting an earlier session's rounding convention (round up to the nearest whole dollar). This item files at $0.98 (round to the nearest cent, per the convention used in more recent sessions — e.g. 1267140, 3020477). The $0.02 difference is a filing-convention artifact only, not a cost difference — both figures round from the same underlying §25 canonical calculation (~$0.98) at the same 0.503 sq ft footprint. This does not affect the tier table, which is priced at exact parity with 1068270 regardless of which material-cost filing convention is used.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-07-01 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves). Final price locked by Nick.**
>
> **This is NOT a direct parity exemption.** P/N 1073950 is an independently validated singles-band data point that converged on exact parity with P/N 1068270 through evidence (Wave 1 build, Wave 2 destruction, Wave 3 buyer simulation, Wave 4 synthesis) — not through a parity-exemption skip. See `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md` for the distinction between a direct parity exemption (no AI validation run, validation inherited from a source item under that exemption) and this item, which ran the full process and arrived at parity through independent validation.

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing and Spec Summary

**Item:** 1073950, CHART-TOP MOUNT JIB 500# G85 (80°) BASKET 500#. 7.25" × 10.00" = 0.503 sq ft. Single label, 1 piece. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope routing (Decision Tree Step 1a from `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`):**

| Threshold | Value | Result |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| sq ft < 0.1? | 0.503 ≥ 0.1 | NOT Micro-Format Band |
| 0.1 ≤ sq ft < 0.5? | 0.503 ≥ 0.5 | NOT Sub-scope |
| sq ft ≥ 0.5? | 0.503 ≥ 0.5 | **SINGLES BAND** |

**Routing: Singles Band (≥0.5 sq ft).** This item lands at the 0.503 sq ft position already established in the singles band (currently held by P/N 1068270, the governing benchmark).

---

## Step 2 — Governing Benchmark and Comparable Set

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft (qty 20) | Notes |
|------|-------|-----------------|-------------------|-------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root; FA Accepted |
| 1267140 (Near-size comparable) | 0.560 | $8.75 | $15.62 | First interior band point |
| 1068270 (Governing benchmark) | 0.503 | $8.00 | $15.91 | Identical area, identical G85 Basket 500# load rating |
| **1073950 (this item)** | **0.503** | **$8.00** | **$15.90** | **Exact parity with 1068270** |

**Key structural observations:**
1. **1068270 is the governing benchmark**, not 1230820 — it shares identical area (0.503 sq ft), identical material family, identical process, and the identical G85 Basket 500# load rating with this item. 1230820 is cited only as the singles band root for context.
2. **Identical area to an already-quoted item is the central fact of this pricing decision.** Wave 1 explored two paths (proportional scaling from the root vs. direct nearest-neighbor parity); Wave 2 through Wave 4 unanimously converged on parity as the only defensible position (see Step 4).
3. **$15.90/sq ft at qty 20 lands inside the existing singles band ($15.43–$15.91/sq ft) — no band recalculation required.** The trivial $0.01/sq ft difference from 1068270's displayed $15.91/sq ft is a rounding-presentation artifact of dividing $8.00/0.503 (both are the same $8.00 price at the same 0.503 sq ft); it is not a price difference.

---

## Step 3 — Material Cost Validation

**Material cost per §25 canonical formula:**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.503 × $1.21 | $0.6086 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.503 × $0.2389 | $0.1202 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 0.503 × $0.50 | $0.2515 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.9803** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) | judgment-applied | − $0.0003 |
| **Canonical material cost** | | **$0.98** |

Material cost is effectively identical to 1068270's ($0.9803 calculated on both items — the $0.98 vs $1.00 filed difference is a buffer-filing-convention artifact, documented in Nesting and Material Cost above). No cost-side reason exists for these two items to price differently.

---

## Step 4 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave. Atomic session model: each prompt self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Nick is sole decision-maker; the responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build Round — independent anchors, no cross-model exposure):** 6/6 models built pricing independently from this item's own specs (0.503 sq ft, Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam, full bleed ink, 1 lam pass), the singles-band Pricing Profile, and the existing 0.503 sq ft precedent already on file. The panel split: **4/6 landed at $7.75/ea**, reasoning from proportional scaling off the root benchmark 1230820 ($15.43/sq ft × 0.503 sq ft ≈ $7.76, rounded to $7.75 by these models). **2/6 landed at $8.00/ea**, anchoring directly to the existing 0.503 sq ft position already on file — the band-ceiling / small-format-premium argument that nearest-neighbor precedent should govern over root-benchmark proportional scaling.

**Wave 2 (Destruction Round):** **Unanimous 6/6 verdict — modify to $8.00/ea, exact parity with P/N 1068270.** The Buyer/Procurement attack, the Cost Auditor attack, and the Strategic-Precedent attack — all three at **High severity**, independently, across different models — converged on the identical structural finding: this item shares **identical area (0.503 sq ft)** with an item already quoted in the catalog (1068270). Any price below the established $8.00 nearest-neighbor rate cannot be defended if Sean ever compares the two drawings side by side — the $7.75 proportional-scaling figure, while mathematically defensible in isolation against the root benchmark, creates a live audit trail showing Pro Label pricing two identical-footprint labels differently with no operational or material justification. **Verdict: modify to $8.00, exact parity with 1068270.** No dissent.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation as Sean Finn):** **Unanimous 6/6.** Every simulation approved $8.00 at qty 20 with **zero price friction**. The single friction point raised — in all six simulations — was not about price at all: is 1073950 distinct artwork from 1068270 (Top Mount Jib vs EZR mount, same G85 Basket 500# load rating), or the same physical label under a different mount designation? All six models flagged this as a clarification worth confirming with Sean directly, but **none treated it as a blocker to sending the quote at $8.00.**

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis, cold atomic sessions with full dossier):** **Unanimous 6/6 YES — send as shown, no tier modifications.** Every model independently concluded that at identical area (0.503 sq ft) and identical G85 Basket 500# load rating, parity with 1068270 is not merely defensible but structurally necessary — deviating from $8.00 in either direction (up toward a small-format premium, or down toward the root-benchmark proportional rate) would reintroduce the exact inconsistency the Wave 2 attacks flagged as a procurement-audit vulnerability.

**Nick's decision:** Locked the full tier table at exact parity with 1068270 — $16.50 / $10.50 / $8.00 / $6.25 / $5.25 / $4.25 — per Wave 4 unanimous consensus. **No override type — engine consensus accepted.** Locked 2026-07-01.

---

## Step 5 — Full Tier Table (Parity Confirmation)

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin (Material-Only) | Source |
|------|------------|-----------|------------------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $16.50 | $32.80 | ~94.1% | Exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 4 unanimous |
| 10-19 | $10.50 | $20.87 | ~90.7% | Exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 4 unanimous |
| 20-49 | $8.00 | $15.90 | ~87.8% | Exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 2/3/4 unanimous |
| 50-99 | $6.25 | $12.43 | ~84.3% | Exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 4 unanimous |
| 100-199 | $5.25 | $10.44 | ~81.3% | Exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 4 unanimous |
| 200+ | $4.25 | $8.45 | ~76.9% | Exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 4 unanimous |

Every tier is dollar-for-dollar identical to P/N 1068270. Minor $/sq ft display differences of a penny or less (e.g. $15.90 here vs $15.91 shown on 1068270) are long-division rounding-presentation artifacts of the same $8.00 ÷ 0.503 sq ft calculation — not price differences.

---

## Step 6 — §26 Tier Boundary Verification

| Boundary | Smaller Qty Total | Larger Qty Total | Cliff | Resolution |
|----------|--------------------|--------------------|-------|-----------|
| 9/10 | 9 × $16.50 = $148.50 | 10 × $10.50 = $105.00 | $43.50 | §26: buyer charged $105.00 for qty 9 |
| 19/20 | 19 × $10.50 = $199.50 | 20 × $8.00 = $160.00 | $39.50 | §26: buyer charged $160.00 for qty 19 |
| 49/50 | 49 × $8.00 = $392.00 | 50 × $6.25 = $312.50 | $79.50 | §26: buyer charged $312.50 for qty 49 |
| 99/100 | 99 × $6.25 = $618.75 | 100 × $5.25 = $525.00 | $93.75 | §26: buyer charged $525.00 for qty 99 |
| 199/200 | 199 × $5.25 = $1,044.75 | 200 × $4.25 = $850.00 | $194.75 | §26: buyer charged $850.00 for qty 199 |

All five tier boundaries resolve cleanly under §26 — identical boundary math to 1068270 (same tier table).

---

## Step 7 — Order Context

No current PO. Order quantity not yet specified by Sean. Quote should reference the standard six-tier table directly. If Sean later requests a one-off small-run order on 1073950, price it fresh from job economics (per §28 no-floor doctrine per `PRICING_RULES.md` §28) rather than assuming the production tier table applies at any quantity.

|----------|-------|----------|---------------|-------------------------|
| 1-9 | $16.50 | $0.98 | $15.52 | ~94.1% |

### Per-Sq-Ft Comparison

|------|---------------------|---------------------|

| 1267140 (interior point) | $15.62 | +1.2% above root |
| 1068270 (governing benchmark) | $15.91 | +3.1% above root |
| **1073950 (this item)** | **$15.90** | **+3.1% above root — exact parity with 1068270** |

### Why the Filed Material Cost Differs Slightly from 1068270's

1068270 files material cost at $1.00 (an earlier session's round-up-to-the-dollar buffer convention); this item files at $0.98 (round-to-the-cent convention used in more recent sessions). > **4-ROUND AI VALIDATION: COMPLETE.** 6 models × 4 rounds = 24 total model runs. Validation modified the tier table and current-order price. See Step 4 below for full documentation.

---

## Step 1 — Spec Summary and Band Classification

- Dimensions: 10" × 7.25" = 0.503 sq ft. Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. 1 production pass.
- At the low end of the singles band scope (~0.5–2.0 sq ft). Above the tiny-label threshold (<0.1 sq ft). The singles per-sq-ft band applies.
- Material cost: $1.00/label (§25 canonical full-bleed formula — color-independent; see color conflict below for the production-setup question).

---

## Step 2 — Singles Band Rate

The singles Pricing Profile has 1 confirmed data point:

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | Rate |
|------|-------|----------------|------|
| 1230820 (Load Chart, D105) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43/sq ft |

**Band width:** Wide (1 data point). Future items in the 0.5–2.0 sq ft range should land within ±15% of $15.43/sq ft ($13.12–$17.74).

Scaling 1082570 at 0.503 sq ft:

`0.503 sq ft × $15.43/sq ft = $7.76` → rounded to **$8.00 at qty 20**

Per-sq-ft check: $8.00 / 0.503 = **$15.91/sq ft** — within ±15% of band center ✓

---

## Step 3 — Full Tier Table Construction

Tier structure proportioned from P/N 1230820's accepted tier ratios relative to the qty 20 anchor:

| Tier | 1230820 Ratio | 1082570 Raw | Quoted |
|------|--------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $30/$20 = 1.50× | $8 × 1.50 = $12.00 | **$12** |
| 10-19 | $24/$20 = 1.20× | $8 × 1.20 = $9.60 | **$10** |
| 20-49 | 1.00× (anchor) | $8.00 | **$8** |
| 50-99 | $17/$20 = 0.85× | $8 × 0.85 = $6.80 | **$7** |
| 100-199 | $14/$20 = 0.70× | $8 × 0.70 = $5.60 | **$6** |
| 200+ | $11/$20 = 0.55× | $8 × 0.55 = $4.40 | **$5** (rounded up — see note) |

**200+ tier note (pre-validation):** $4.40 proportionally rounds to $4. However, at material cost $1.70, a $4 tier yields ~58% margin — below the category minimum-worthwhile floor (~64%). Preliminary: rounded up to $5. See Step 4 — AI validation replaced this tier table with final validated figures.

**This is the pre-validation preliminary tier table.** AI validation (Step 4) modified all tiers — particularly 1-9 (raised from $12 to $16.50), 50-99 (reduced from $7 to $6.25), 100-199 (reduced from $6 to $5.25), and 200+ (reduced from $5 to $4.25 as volume reward for strategic account). The qty 20-49 anchor ($8.00) was confirmed unchanged.

---

## Step 4 — Current Order Pricing: Option A vs Option B + AI Validation (4 Rounds, 6 Models)

### The Core Question

At qty 2, what price does this order receive?

**Option A:** Apply the $55 account minimum-worthwhile-charge floor ($55.00 total, $27.50/label implied).

**Option B:** Price on job economics with setup recovery — a flat rate anchored to what the job warrants at this label size and order quantity.

**Option A argument:** Consistent with prior one-off small-run orders on this account (P/N 1277970-1278000 program, P/N 3017583, P/N 3017584).

**Option B argument:** Those precedents are tiny-label one-offs (0.008–0.026 sq ft) where the per-sq-ft band is inapplicable. At 0.503 sq ft, 1082570 is a full singles-band label — Sean can make a direct D105 comparison. A $55 total ($27.50/label implied) = $54.67/sq ft, vs D105 at $15.43/sq ft. That is 3.5× the benchmark rate and is indefensible if Sean runs the math.

---

### Round 1 — Build (6 Models)

- Qty 2 consensus: **$38–$45 cluster**
- Qty 20 consensus: **$8.50–$11.00 cluster**
- **All 6 rejected the $55 floor** for a 0.503 sq ft label vs D105 comparison
- All 6 agreed setup-recovery logic applies to the current order, not sq ft band alone
- All 6 confirmed the 20-49 anchor at approximately $8.00–$9.00/label

---

### Round 2 — Destruction (6 Models)

- **All 6 found the $55 floor indefensible** at this label size — D105 comparison available and damaging
- All 6 identified the 20-49 tier as most competitively exposed vs D105
- **All 6 recommended Option B** (job economics) over Option A ($55 floor)
- Consensus qty 2 total: **$40–$42**
- Consensus qty 20 tier: **$7.25–$8.50/label**
- Consensus 1-9 tier: **$14–$18/label**
  - One model specifically recommended $16.50 to widen the gap between the flat current-order price and the low-end catalog tier — noted for Round 4

---

### Round 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 Models)

- **5 of 6 models approved $42 for qty 2 without question** — instant approval below $50–$100 threshold
- D105 comparison at qty 20: $15.91/sq ft vs $15.43/sq ft = **3% delta** — every model called it a non-issue
- Job economics explanation (setup recovery on a 2-label run) was the **single most cited approval driver** across all 6 models
- Mental model Sean builds: ~$15.50/sq ft at qty 20, $40–$55 short-run floor — healthy framework for future quotes
- 1 outlier recommended $24–$26 total — **rejected**; ignores setup recovery and undercuts the account's own value structure

---

### Round 4 — Final Synthesis (6 Models)

- **5 of 6 unanimous: send as shown, no changes**
- 1 model recommended bumping the 1-9 tier from $16.00 to **$16.50** to widen the gap at low quantities — **adopted**
- No tier flagged as wrong by majority
- Long-term precedent: holds cleanly for 20+ items on this account without creating pricing friction
- No discomfort flags on any tier or on the $42 flat current-order price
- **No MOQ.** One model raised the possibility of a minimum order quantity. Nick's decision: **no MOQ on this account**. Elliott is a strategic account with major growth potential — access at any quantity is intentional and permanent.

---

### Final Validated Tier Table

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin | Notes |
|------|------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| 1-9 | $16.50 | $32.80 | ~94% | Widened from preliminary $12 |
| 10-19 | $10.50 | $20.88 | ~90% | |
| 20-49 | $8.00 | $15.91 | ~88% | Band anchor confirmed — D105 delta +3%, non-issue |
| 50-99 | $6.25 | $12.43 | ~84% | |
| 100-199 | $5.25 | $10.44 | ~81% | |
| 200+ | $4.25 | $8.45 | ~76% | Intentionally steepest volume discount — reward for strategic account |

**200+ tier:** $8.45/sq ft is intentionally below the D105 anchor ($15.43/sq ft). Volume reward for a strategic account — Nick's decision. The margin floor reflects the steepest tier discount by design. Not a margin error; documented as a strategic override. (Margins updated 2026-06-09 — material cost normalized to the §25 canonical formula, $1.00 vs the prior $1.33 Safety Yellow flood-coat assumption; tier prices unchanged.)

**No MOQ:** No minimum order quantity has been set, and none should ever be set on this account, this item, or any future Elliott item. This is a permanent account-level rule — do not add, suggest, or imply MOQ language anywhere.

**The $42.00 current-order price does NOT enter the singles Pricing Profile band.** The band tracks production-volume acceptance. One-off tied-ship orders are excluded from band calculations.

---

## Step 5 — Color Version and the §25 Ink Standard

The open color conflict (Safety Yellow flood coat per drawing note 6 vs black-on-white per artwork) is a **production-setup question only**. Under the account-wide §25 full-bleed standard (established 2026-06-01, applied to this item 2026-06-09), ink is priced at $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft regardless of color. Material cost ($1.00/label) and every tier price are identical under either version. No recosting or reprice is triggered by Sean's selection.

Details in Nesting and Material Cost section.

---

## Step 6 — Job Economics for Current Order ($42.00 Flat)

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Quantity | 2 labels |
| Material cost | 2 × $1.00 = $2.00 |
| File prep / artwork prep / design | $0 — Elliott provides production-ready DWG (account rule, `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Estimated production time | ~20-25 min |
| Quote price (job economics / setup recovery) | $42.00 |
| Revenue per production minute | ~$1.68–$2.10/min (~$101–$126/hr) |
| Contribution margin on this order | ($42 − $2.00) / $42 = **~95%** |

The ~95% contribution margin is structural to job-economics pricing on a small-material-cost 2-label run. It reflects contribution to setup recovery and fixed overhead, not excess profit. Effective job-level margin is lower when all fixed costs are allocated. The $42.00 is intentionally above the 1-9 tier math ($16.50 × 2 = $33.00) to reflect the setup-recovery component on this specific tied ship order.

---

>
> No dimensions appear on the drawing (dated 08-25-04). Width 10" and Height 7.25" were confirmed by Derrik Walton, P.E., Engineering Manager at Elliott Equipment Company, via email on 2026-05-28. Walton referenced the I50 chart as the size standard for the I70 model.
>

---

> ## MYLAR TERMINOLOGY
>
> Drawing calls out "1 mil clear Mylar overlay." Mylar is a DuPont-originated trade name for biaxially oriented polyester (BoPET) film. It maps directly to the standard 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate used across the entire Orajet 3951 material family on this account. No material substitution. No ambiguity.
>
> Consistent with the same terminology note documented on P/N 3017584 (same "Mylar overlay" wording). Nick is actively working with Elliott to standardize spec-sheet verbiage toward "1-mil polyester overlaminate."

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE
>
> 4-round, 6-model AI validation (24 total model runs per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`) was completed following the initial quote session. Full documentation in Pricing Derivation, Step 4.
>
> **Key outcomes:** Per-label catalog rate at qty 2 rejected by all 6 models — $42 flat adopted for job economics / setup recovery. Tier table modified from preliminary: $16.50 / $10.50 / $8.00 / $6.25 / $5.25 / $4.25. No MOQ — Nick's decision, permanent account rule.

---

> ## STRATEGIC ACCOUNT DEVELOPMENT — ENGINEERING/PROCUREMENT STANDARD PROJECT
>
> Sean's response to the physical sample delivery went beyond color selection. He asked Nick to suggest what specs to call out on a drawing for label/decal procurement.
>
> More significantly: **Sean and his manager are initiating a project to develop an engineering and procurement standard for labels and decals at Elliott Equipment Company.** They will be reaching out to Nick for input on the standard.
>
> **This is not a pricing input.** It is documented here as strategic account context for anyone working this account in the future:
> - Elliott is actively building a procurement standard for this product category — Pro Label has an opportunity to help define it.
> - Nick's involvement at the engineering/specification level positions Pro Label as the expert vendor of record, not a commodity supplier.
> - This initiative is separate from the current color conflict resolution. It is a long-term relationship signal.
> - Any future Claude Code session working this account should be aware of this when framing quotes, communications, or strategic recommendations.
>
> **No action required on pricing or production.** Update this note when Sean's outreach occurs and the scope of the standard project becomes clearer.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 4.00" width and 4.75" height dimensions clear the 13.5" polyester laminator with
wide margin (8.75"–9.50" clearance either way). Single lamination pass in either
orientation. No laminator complications.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 4.00" × 4.75". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

- **Orientation A** (4.00" across feed, 4.75" in feed direction): 4.00" across the 28"
  print bed → **7 labels across, exactly filling the bed** (7 × 4.00" = 28.00" ≤ 28",
  zero waste).
- **Orientation B** (4.75" across feed, 4.00" in feed direction): 4.75" across the 28" bed
  → 5 labels across (5 × 4.75" = 23.75" ≤ 28"; a 6th would require 28.5", which does not
  fit).

Orientation A (7-across, zero-waste) is the optimal print layout at any production
quantity. Orientation is a nesting-efficiency choice, not a forced constraint — both
dimensions independently clear the laminator.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label — §25 Canonical Formula

> Material cost version date: 2026-07-14. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md`
> (Orajet verified 2026-04-22; laminate verified 2026-05-28). Full bleed ink rule per
> `PRICING_RULES.md` §25.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.132 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.1597 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.132 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.0315 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed per §25) | 0.132 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.0660 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.2572** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) ¹ | rounds up to the cent | + $0.0028 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.26** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per `PRICING_RULES.md` §25):** the
calculated material total is filed to the nearest cent per account convention — here
$0.2572 files at $0.26, a conventional round-to-the-cent, well within the documented §25
compliance tolerance used across this category (compare 1247120: calculated $0.2377 filed
$0.25; 3020477: calculated $0.2534 filed $0.25).

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-07-14 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses,
> 6 models × 4 waves). Final price locked by Nick.**
>
> **Do not cite P/N 1210810 as a benchmark or precedent anywhere in this file.** It is on
> the account's `do_not_benchmark` list (sub-scope single, not production-volume
> accepted). Wave 1 initially over-relied on it; Waves 2–4 corrected for that — that
> correction is real validation history and is documented below, but 1210810 is not used
> as a cited comp for the final number.

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing and Spec Summary

**Item:** 1101250, LBL-DNGR MAX PLAT. 2100. 4.00" × 4.75" = 0.132 sq ft. Single label, 1
piece. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. 1 production pass. ANSI danger-class label (§29
account rule).

**Dimensional scope routing (Decision Tree Step 1a from `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`):**

| Threshold | Value | Result |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| sq ft < 0.1? | 0.132 ≥ 0.1 | NOT Micro-Format Band |
| 0.1 ≤ sq ft < 0.5? | 0.132 is in range | **SUB-SCOPE HANDLING** |
| sq ft ≥ 0.5? | 0.132 < 0.5 | NOT Singles Band |

**Routing: Sub-scope (0.1–0.5 sq ft).** Below the calibrated singles-band floor (~0.5 sq
ft, calibrated 0.503–1.296 sq ft). Priced band-consistent with a small-format premium per
the sub-scope doctrine (governing precedent: 1247120, 1279130, 3020477, 3024595,
3017572) — excluded from official band data points until production-volume acceptance,
same status as P/N 1210810.

---

## Step 2 — Governing Benchmark and Per-Tier Comparison

**Governing benchmark: P/N 1230820** (ROOT BENCHMARK, FA Accepted, 1.296 sq ft, full tier
table: $30/$24/$20/$17/$14/$11).

This derivation compares this item's $/sq ft at **each tier** to 1230820's $/sq ft at the
**same tier** (not only the flat qty-20 rate), because the Wave 4 dissent's "U-shaped
premium curve" concern and Nick's final ruling both turn on the shape of that curve across
all six tiers:

| Tier | 1230820 Price | 1230820 $/Sq Ft | 1101250 Price | 1101250 $/Sq Ft | Premium |
|------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------|
| 1-9 | $30.00 | $23.15 | $4.00 | $30.30 | +30.9% |
| 10-19 | $24.00 | $18.52 | $3.00 | $22.73 | +22.7% |
| 20-49 | $20.00 | $15.43 | $2.25 | $17.05 | **+10.5%** |
| 50-99 | $17.00 | $13.12 | $1.75 | $13.26 | +1.1% |
| 100-199 | $14.00 | $10.80 | $1.50 | $11.36 | +5.2% |
| 200+ | $11.00 | $8.49 | $1.25 | $9.47 | +11.6% |

**Key structural observations:**
1. **1230820 is the sole governing benchmark.** P/N 1210810 is explicitly excluded from
   this comparison per the account's `do_not_benchmark` list.
2. **The premium curve is U-shaped, not monotonic:** it narrows from +10.5% (20-49) down to
   +1.1% (50-99), rises slightly to +5.2% (100-199), then widens to +11.6% (200+). This is
   the shape the Wave 4 dissent flagged as worth smoothing (see Step 4).
3. **$17.05/sq ft at qty 20-49 = +10.5% over the 1230820 benchmark ($15.43/sq ft)** — a
   defensible, deliberate small-format premium, not benchmark parity and not an outlier
   spike.

---

## Step 3 — Material Cost Validation

**Material cost per §25 canonical formula:**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.132 × $1.21 | $0.1597 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.132 × $0.2389 | $0.0315 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 0.132 × $0.50 | $0.0660 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.2572** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) | rounds up to the cent | + $0.0028 |
| **Canonical material cost** | | **$0.26** |

Material cost is negligible at this size class (6.9%–20.8% of price across the six tiers)
— labor and fixed process cost dominate, consistent with every other sub-scope item on
file.

---

## Step 4 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`.
6 top-tier models per wave. Atomic session model: each prompt self-contained; no model has
visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Nick is sole decision-maker; the
responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build Round — independent anchors, no cross-model exposure):** Qty-20 prices
ranged **$2.35–$2.75** across the 6 models, center of mass ~$2.50. **Process finding, not a
benchmark citation:** all 6 models anchored heavily on P/N 1210810 (0.292 sq ft, an account
`do_not_benchmark` item) when building their initial number. This is flagged here as the
reason Wave 2 corrected course — it is real validation history, but 1210810 is not treated
as a comp anywhere in the final pricing logic.

**Wave 2 (Destruction Round):** **5 of 6 models returned "No" verdicts on the $2.50
draft.** Two unanimous High-severity findings drove the correction:
- **Cost Auditor (High, unanimous):** the small-format handling premium embedded in the
  $2.50 draft is *modeled*, not *measured* — no production-time data exists for sub-0.15
  sq ft ANSI labels on this account. The premium was asserted, not derived from an
  observed cycle time.
- **Strategic (High, unanimous):** this item becomes the account's **sole official
  reference point below the 0.5 sq ft band floor** once filed, and Sean will cite it for
  future micro-format quotes — the number has to be defensible as a permanent floor, not
  just a one-off.

One dissenting model argued for pricing **higher** ($2.75) specifically to avoid setting a
ceiling too low for future interpolation — a real, considered minority position, not noise,
carried forward into Wave 3. Consensus converged on **$2.00–$2.25 at 20-49**, with
**$1.75/$1.50/$1.25 at 50-99/100-199/200+** independently proposed by multiple models.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation as Sean Finn):** **5 of 6 simulated responses approved the
refined $2.25 table and sent the PO as-is** — no phone call, no email. **1 dissent** (the
same model that drove Wave 2's harshest findings) wanted recalibration down to **$1.95**.
Strong independent consensus across the panel: **pushback threshold ~$2.50/label
(+22–23% over benchmark)**; **instant-approval number ~$2.00/label (benchmark parity)**.
Multiple models explicitly flagged that pricing **below** $2.00 would be the **worse**
long-term outcome — it anchors a floor Sean defends indefinitely, compressing all future
small-format quotes downward.

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis, cold atomic sessions with full dossier):** **5 of 6 YES, send
as shown, no tier modifications.** **1 dissent** proposed raising 50-99/100-199/200+ by
$0.25 each ($2.00/$1.75/$1.50) to smooth what it called a "U-shaped premium curve" — per
Step 2's per-tier comparison, the as-shown table's premium narrows to +1.1%–+5.2% at
50-199 before widening to +11.6% at 200+ (the model rounded this to "+1–5%" and "+11.5%"
in its discussion).

**Nick's decision:** Locked the original table as shown — **$4.00 / $3.00 / $2.25 / $1.75 /
$1.50 / $1.25.** Reasoning: Sean normalizes per-item against the single official benchmark
(1230820's qty-20 $/sq ft), not against a cross-tier premium curve he has no visibility
into; the §30 $0.25-increment constraint makes the dissent's proposed fix **worse, not
better** — recomputing the alternative table ($2.00/$1.75/$1.50) against 1230820's
per-tier $/sq ft shows it would push the 200+ tier to **+33.9% (~+34%)** over benchmark
instead of the as-shown table's **+11.6%** — a larger and more visible number at a tier
Sean is unlikely to ever transact (order pattern is batches of 20–50; see
`governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 deep-tier standing instruction). **No override on the
20-49 anchor tier — engine consensus (Wave 4 majority) accepted as-is at $2.25.**

---

## Step 5 — §31 Sub-Scope Root Floor: One-Time Exception on the Deep Tiers

`governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 establishes that the $15.43/sq ft root floor (P/N
1230820) is a hard floor at **every** quantity tier on sub-scope items, not merely the
qty-20 rate used for band positioning — established 2026-07-01, forward-looking from that
date.

At 0.132 sq ft, the absolute floor price is 0.132 × $15.43 = **$2.04** per label. Checked
against the locked table:

| Tier | Price | $/Sq Ft | ≥ $15.43/sq ft floor? |
|------|-------|---------|------------------------|
| 1-9 | $4.00 | $30.30 | Yes |
| 10-19 | $3.00 | $22.73 | Yes |
| 20-49 | $2.25 | $17.05 | Yes |
| 50-99 | $1.75 | $13.26 | **No — below floor** |
| 100-199 | $1.50 | $11.36 | **No — below floor** |
| 200+ | $1.25 | $9.47 | **No — below floor** |

The 50-99, 100-199, and 200+ tiers price below the §31 absolute floor. This is classified
as a **Nick-directed One-Time Exception per Core Rule 12/13** — non-precedent-setting.
Consistent with the P/N 3017572 precedent (the first tested §31 exception, 2026-07-10,
where the 100-199/200+ tiers were similarly overridden below the floor). Per Core Rule 13,
this override does not weaken §31 itself, and future sub-scope items — particularly in the
sub-0.15 sq ft range — must benchmark their deep tiers against the **validated $15.43/sq
ft floor**, not against this item's overridden $1.75/$1.50/$1.25 figures.

**No model at any wave proposed a 50-99/100-199/200+ tier below $15.43/sq ft as a
validated recommendation** — the values that survived Wave 2–4 consensus ($1.75/$1.50/
$1.25) were independently proposed on their own terms (job economics at this size class,
consistency with the $/sq ft curve shape), not benchmarked against §31 at the time. Nick's
final lock accepts these values as an explicit, logged exception rather than requiring a
flat-tier structure (per the 3024595 precedent) or a One-Time Exception at a different set
of tiers.

---

## Step 6 — Full Tier Table with Margin and §26 Verification

| Tier | Price/Label | Per Sq Ft | Margin (Material-Only) | Source |
|------|------------|-----------|--------------------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $4.00 | $30.30 | ~93.5% | Wave 4 majority (5/6 YES) |
| 10-19 | $3.00 | $22.73 | ~91.3% | Wave 4 majority (5/6 YES) |
| 20-49 | $2.25 | $17.05 | ~88.4% | Wave 4 majority (5/6 YES) — Nick locked |
| 50-99 | $1.75 | $13.26 | ~85.1% | Wave 2-4 consensus; §31 One-Time Exception |
| 100-199 | $1.50 | $11.36 | ~82.7% | Wave 2-4 consensus; §31 One-Time Exception |
| 200+ | $1.25 | $9.47 | ~79.2% | Wave 2-4 consensus; §31 One-Time Exception |

**§26 Tier Boundary Verification:**

| Boundary | Smaller Qty Total | Larger Qty Total | Cliff | Resolution |
|----------|--------------------|--------------------|-------|-----------|
| 9/10 | 9 × $4.00 = $36.00 | 10 × $3.00 = $30.00 | $6.00 | §26: buyer charged $30.00 for qty 9 |
| 19/20 | 19 × $3.00 = $57.00 | 20 × $2.25 = $45.00 | $12.00 | §26: buyer charged $45.00 for qty 19 |
| 49/50 | 49 × $2.25 = $110.25 | 50 × $1.75 = $87.50 | $22.75 | §26: buyer charged $87.50 for qty 49 |
| 99/100 | 99 × $1.75 = $173.25 | 100 × $1.50 = $150.00 | $23.25 | §26: buyer charged $150.00 for qty 99 |
| 199/200 | 199 × $1.50 = $298.50 | 200 × $1.25 = $250.00 | $48.50 | §26: buyer charged $250.00 for qty 199 |

All five tier boundaries resolve cleanly under §26.

---

## Step 7 — Order Context

No current PO. Order quantity not specified by Sean this session — quote references the
standard six-tier table directly. First article not requested. If Sean later requests a
one-off small-run order on 1101250, price it fresh from job economics (per §28 no-floor
doctrine) rather than assuming the production tier table applies at any quantity.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 7.25" width and 10.00" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Single lamination pass in either orientation. No laminator complications.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 7.25" × 10.00". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

- **Orientation A** (7.25" across feed, 10.00" in feed direction): 7.25" across the 28" print bed → up to 3 labels across (3 × 7.25" = 21.75" ≤ 28").
- **Orientation B** (10.00" across feed, 7.25" in feed direction): 10.00" across the 28" bed → up to 2 labels across (2 × 10.00" = 20" ≤ 28").

Nesting is identical to P/N 1068270, P/N 1073950, and P/N 1062390 — Orientation A (3-across) is the optimal layout at higher quantities; both orientations fit cleanly at low quantities.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label — §25 Canonical Formula

> Material cost version date: 2026-07-14. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md`. Full bleed ink rule per `PRICING_RULES.md` §25.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.503 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.6086 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.503 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.1202 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed per §25) | 0.503 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.2515 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.9803** |
| **Filed `material_cost_per_unit`** (round to nearest cent — current account convention) | | **$0.98** |

Material cost matches P/N 1073950's and P/N 1062390's filed cost exactly ($0.98/label — all three round the identical $0.9803 calculation to the nearest cent). P/N 1068270 files at $1.00/label under an earlier round-to-the-dollar buffer convention — a filing-convention artifact only, not a cost difference; all four items derive from the same underlying §25 canonical calculation at 0.503 sq ft.

>
> **No wave validation was run this session.** The benchmark is P/N 1068270, with parity independently reconfirmed by P/N 1073950's 4-wave AI validation (unanimous 6/6 on exact parity with 1068270) and by P/N 1062390's direct-parity filing (no re-validation, same precedent). This item inherits both precedent chains.

**Model/load-class note:** 1132950 (I50, Platform 600#) differs from the other three (G85/G85R, Basket 500#) in model designation and load rating — a bigger artwork difference than the mount-only variation between 1068270 and 1073950. This does not affect parity: cost is driven by dimensions, material stack, and process — all identical — not by load-chart content. Full-bleed ink is priced flat at $0.50/sq ft regardless of what's printed, consistent with how 1068270's and 1082570's differing artwork (G85 vs I70 load charts) never affected their shared pricing. This distinction is flagged in Notes and Warnings for transparency; it does not block the exemption.

## Step 3 — Pricing Profile Band Check (Rule 15 — still applies)

The category singles band at qty 20 is **$15.43–$15.91/sq ft** (calibrated at the 0.503–1.296 sq ft range).

1132950 at qty 20: $8.00 / 0.503 = **$15.90/sq ft** — lands cleanly within the singles band, matching 1073950's and 1062390's displayed rate exactly (the $0.01 difference from 1068270's $15.91/sq ft is a long-division rounding-presentation artifact of the identical $8.00 ÷ 0.503 calculation).

Rule 15 satisfied. No band adjustment required. This is **not a new independent band data point** — the 0.503 sq ft position remains anchored where it already was, now shared by four items (1068270, 1073950, 1062390, 1132950).

## Step 4 — Why No AI Validation Was Run This Session

Per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md`: "When NOT to use it: Items dimensionally and materially identical to something already quoted. Match the existing structure and move on."

1132950 meets every dimensional and material parity criterion against 1068270, 1073950, and 1062390. There is no new pricing question to validate, no new size class, no new material, no new process, and no new tier structure. Two independent pricing paths already converged on this exact tier table at this exact footprint, and a third item (1062390) was already filed under the same exemption:

1. **1068270** was priced via direct parity exemption from 1082570 (its own precedent chain).
2. **1073950** independently ran the full 4-wave, 6-model AI validation process (24 responses) and converged unanimous 6/6 on exact parity with 1068270 — Wave 2 (Destruction) specifically found that High-severity Buyer/Procurement, Cost Auditor, and Strategic-Precedent attacks all converge on the same finding: identical area to an already-quoted item makes parity the only defensible position.
3. **1062390** was filed under the Direct Parity Exemption at this same tier table with no re-validation, citing 1073950's precedent.

Cloning the validated tier table a fourth time at this same footprint is the procedurally correct action — running a fresh 4-wave validation would only re-derive a conclusion already reached three times over. **1073950's Pricing Derivation (reinforced by 1062390's filing) is the governing precedent for this exemption.** The model/load-class distinction (I50 Platform 600# vs G85/G85R Basket 500#) does not change this analysis — dimensional and material identity, not artwork content, governs the exemption.

They have not.

**Filing-convention note:** if margin were instead computed against 1068270's $1.00 filed material cost (round-to-dollar convention rather than round-to-cent), margin at qty 20 reads ~87.5% and at 200+ reads ~76.5% — the ~87.4–89.6% range cited in the session prompt spans these two filing conventions plus the 1-9 tier extremes (~94.1% at $0.98 cost vs ~93.9% at $1.00 cost). Both conventions derive from the same $0.9803 canonical calculation at 0.503 sq ft; only the display differs. This item files at $0.98 (round-to-cent), matching 1073950's and 1062390's convention.

> ## DIRECT PARITY EXEMPTION — NO OVERRIDE
>
> P/N 1132950 is priced via the Direct Parity Exemption per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md` — no multi-round AI validation was run this session. **`override_type` is blank — this is not an override.** No engine recommendation was overridden by Nick; the tier table is cloned directly from the validated precedent chain (1068270's direct-parity origin, independently reconfirmed by 1073950's 4-wave AI validation, and inherited a second time by 1062390's direct-parity filing).

---

> ## FOURTH ITEM AT THE ESTABLISHED 0.503 SQ FT SINGLES-BAND POSITION
>
> P/N 1132950 is the **fourth item** at the established 0.503 sq ft singles-band position, joining P/N 1068270, P/N 1073950, and P/N 1062390. **This item does NOT add a new independent band data point** — the 0.503 sq ft position remains anchored where it already was.

---

> ## MODEL / LOAD-CLASS NOTE — I50 PLATFORM 600# vs G85/G85R BASKET 500# (DOES NOT AFFECT PRICING)
>
> P/N 1132950's model field reads **I50** (drawing title: CHRT JIB-I50 TP JIB 500# PLATFORM 600#), distinct from the plain **G85** model designation on P/N 1068270 (Basket Jib chart) and P/N 1073950 (Top Mount Jib chart), and distinct from the **G85R** designation on P/N 1062390 (Working Range chart). Unlike the mount-only variation between 1068270 and 1073950 (same G85 model, same Basket 500# load class, different mount configuration), P/N 1132950 differs on **both** the model family (I50 vs G85/G85R) **and** the load-rating class (Platform 600# / Top Jib 500# vs Basket 500#) — a materially bigger artwork difference than any pairing seen so far at this footprint.
>
> **This does not affect pricing in any way.** Cost is driven by dimensions, material stack, and process — all identical across all four items — not by load-chart content. Full-bleed ink is priced flat at $0.50/sq ft regardless of what's printed, consistent with how 1068270's and 1082570's differing artwork (G85 vs I70 load charts) never affected their shared pricing. Whether the I50/Platform 600# designation reflects a genuinely distinct machine variant requiring separate documentation, or simply a different model in the same label-format family, is **unresolved as of this filing** — flagged here strictly as a taxonomy question for Nick and Sean to resolve alongside the quote, consistent with how 1073950 and 1062390 each flagged their own artwork/model questions. The Direct Parity Exemption governs on dimensional and material identity, not on artwork or model content.

---

> ## NO MULTI-ROUND AI VALIDATION — DIRECT PARITY EXEMPTION
>
> Per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md` direct parity exemption. The tier table is cloned from 1068270 (direct parity origin), independently reconfirmed by 1073950's full 4-wave, 6-model AI validation (24 responses, unanimous 6/6 on exact parity), and inherited a second time by 1062390's direct-parity filing. All three precedent chains are inherited by this item; no new validation session was warranted or run.

---

> ## NO BURDENED-COST DOCTRINE ON THIS ACCOUNT
>
> Every margin figure in this file is material-only. Do not introduce a burdened-cost calculation for this item in a future session without first establishing an account-wide burdened-cost doctrine.

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule (Core Rule 7). See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## FULL BLEED INK STANDARD
>
> Material cost includes full bleed ink coverage at $0.50/sq ft per the account-wide rule (`PRICING_RULES.md` §25). No partial, medium, or low coverage assumptions are permitted on any Elliott printed/laminated item. The $0.98/label material cost reflects the filed total under the canonical formula:
>
> `(Orajet 0.503 × $1.21) + (lam 0.503 × $0.2389) + (ink 0.503 × $0.50) = $0.9803, filed at $0.98`

---

> ## ANSI — ACCOUNT RULE (§29)

> This item is ANSI by account-wide default — all items printed on Orajet 3951 with polyester laminate are ANSI per `PRICING_RULES.md` §29. No separate ANSI floor consideration applies here since this item routes to the singles band (≥0.5 sq ft), not the sub-0.06 sq ft per-label floor tier.
