sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red
- **Drawing Callout (Note 3):** "2-MIL, 3M 180-53 CARDINAL RED, 5-7 YEAR VINYL, ADHESIVE BACK" + "VINYL CUT LETTERING, AND CARRIER FOR APPLICATION"
- **Film Type:** 2-mil cast vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Rated Durability:** 10-year (exceeds drawing spec of 5-7 year)
- **Roll Size:** 24" × 50yd = $775.10/roll ($15.502/yd, $7.751/sq ft) — verified 2026-05-28 (see `materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red.md`)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, 24" × 100yd = $118.21/roll ($1.1821/yd, $0.5911/sq ft) — verified 2026-05-28 (see `materials/transferrite-582u.md`)
- **Laminate:** N/A — cut vinyl, no laminate

> **Note on roll selection:** This item uses the standard 24" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (same roll used by the small-format Band A cluster items 1205720 and 1186310). The 9.38" label height rotated across the 24" roll enables 2-across nesting (2 × 9.38" = 18.76", 5.24" clearance). The 24" TransferRite 582U tape matches the vinyl roll width exactly — no overhang, no waste strip. The 15" Cardinal Red roll used by Band B (3010704) is wrong for this item — at 9.38" height with 2-across nesting, 15" stock would force 1-across only, doubling vinyl cost per label.

---
sections.nesting:
## 90-Degree Rotation Geometry

The label is **49.16" wide × 9.38" tall** when installed. During production the label is **rotated 90 degrees on the roll** so that:

- **9.38" (label height) feeds ACROSS the 24" roll width** — enables 2-across nesting
- **49.16" (label width) feeds ALONG the roll length** — becomes the cut length per row

This is the same length-based, length-feeds-along-roll convention used by every other length-based cut vinyl item on this account (1205720, 3010704, 3010707).

## Roll Specs

| Component | Roll | Cost | Cost/Yard | Cost/Sq Ft |
|-----------|------|------|-----------|------------|
| 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red | 24" × 50yd | $775.10 | $15.502 | $7.751 |
| TransferRite Ultra 582U | 24" × 100yd | $118.21 | $1.1821 | $0.5911 |

## Vinyl Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across roll, rotated) | 9.38" |
| Labels across 24" roll | **2** (2 × 9.38" = 18.76", **5.24" clearance**) |
| Label length (along roll, rotated) | 49.16" |
| Spacing allocation per row | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per row of 2 | 49.16" + 6" = **55.16" = 1.532 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per row of 2 | 1.532 yd × $15.502/yd = $23.749 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $23.749 ÷ 2 = **$11.87** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Tape width | 24" (matches vinyl roll width exactly) |
| Feed length per row of 2 | 1.532 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 2 | 1.532 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.811 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.811 ÷ 2 = **$0.91** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red, 24" × 50yd) | $11.87 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 24" × 100yd) | $0.91 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$12.78** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $12.78 ÷ 3.202 = **$3.99/sq ft**.

## Labels per Roll

- Rows per 50yd roll (1800" usable): floor(1800 / 55.16) = **32 complete rows**
- Labels per roll: 32 × 2 = **64 labels per roll**

---
sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Cardinal Red stock. 2 labels across (2 × 9.38" = 18.76" across 24" roll, 5.24" clearance). Single continuous cut path of the "ELLIOTT" wordmark, repeated 2-up per row.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 7 block characters (E ×3, L ×2, I ×1, O ×1, T ×2 — wait, "ELLIOTT" = E-L-L-I-O-T-T, so E ×1, L ×2, I ×1, O ×1, T ×2). Open counters on E and O make weeding efficient per character; the 49.16" weed span across the row of 2 labels is the dominant time factor.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 49.16" width.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application on crane body/boom.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width — well within tolerance)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low–medium. 7 block characters at medium scale, open counters on E ×1 and O ×1. Geometrically similar to the LRG sibling (3010704), but at smaller scale; comparable to the small-format model designations (1205720 "E190", 1186310 "E160") in character count and shape complexity. The 49.16" weed/mask span is wider than the small-format cluster (~33.5" wide) but narrower than the LRG (~70.8" wide) — fractional width-handling premium of ~0.5% above proportional captures this.
- **Cut feed length:** 55.16" per row of 2 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 32 complete rows × 2 labels = 64 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Sq ft scaling within the Band A (Small-Format, 1.0–5.0 sq ft) Cut Vinyl Profile, anchored to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft small-format cluster ($35/qty 20, $13.67/sq ft) with a 0.5% fractional width-handling premium for the 49.16" weed/mask span. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves) plus a revised Wave 2 with corrected geometry context. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES (6/6) on the tier table as shown.

**Sq ft / $/sq ft comparison across the cut vinyl bands:**

| Band | Representative Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Position |
|------|---------------------|-------|----------------|---------|----------|
| Band B (Large-Format) | 3010704 (LRG sibling) | 7.069 | $78.00 | $11.03 | LRG anchor |
| Band A (Small-Format) | **3010701 (this item — MED, founding 3–5 sq ft data point)** | **3.202** | **$44.00** | **$13.74** | **First Band A item outside 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster** |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1205720 (small-format cluster anchor) | 2.56 | $35.00 | $13.67 | Cluster anchor |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1186310 (small-format cluster, E160) | 2.564 | $35.00 | $13.65 | Cluster |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 3017435 (small-format cluster, ELLIOTT White) | 2.56 | $35.00 | $13.67 | Cluster |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 3018378 (small-format cluster, D115 Olympic Blue) | 2.512 | $35.00 | $13.94 | Cluster |

Monotonically increasing $/sq ft as sq ft drops within Band A; the MED item ($13.74/sq ft) sits inside the Band A range ($13.65–$13.94/sq ft).

**Proportional price scaling math:**

- Area ratio (MED / small-format anchor): 3.202 / 2.56 = **1.251**
- Price ratio (MED / small-format anchor): $44 / $35 = **1.257**
- Differential: 1.257 / 1.251 = **+0.5% premium above proportional** — fractional width-handling premium for the 49.16" weed/mask span. Proportional pricing with a small structural adjustment, not a band shift.

**Family position — ELLIOTT S/M/L wordmark family:**

| P/N | Family Role | Dimensions | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft |
|-----|-------------|------------|-------|----------------|---------|
| (not yet defined) | SML | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| **3010701 (this item)** | **MED** | 49.16" × 9.38" | 3.202 | $44.00 | $13.74 |
| 3010704 | LRG | 70.8125" × 14.375" | 7.069 | $78.00 | $11.03 |

3010701 is confirmed as the middle member of the ELLIOTT S/M/L brand wordmark family. SML is not yet defined; LRG is 3010704. The MED member sits inside Band A; LRG sits inside Band B. The $/sq ft curve is monotonic and structurally coherent.

**LRG inversion floor check (200+ tier):**

The 200+ tier ($39 at 3.202 sq ft = $12.18/sq ft) must stay above the LRG inversion floor — i.e., it must not drop below LRG $/sq ft scaled by the MED area:

- LRG inversion floor: 3.202 × $11.03 = **$35.32 per label**
- MED 200+ tier: **$39.00 per label**
- Headroom: $39.00 − $35.32 = **$3.68 per label** above the floor (10.4% above)

The MED 200+ price is structurally above the LRG band — no $/sq ft inversion at any quantity. The locked tier table preserves the band hierarchy at every tier.

## 4-Wave Validation Record

**Wave 1 — Build Round (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Six models built price from scratch without anchor exposure.
- Cluster: $42.50–$44.00 (4 models). Central tendency: $43.25.
- Outliers rejected: M1 at $33.50 (inverts family curve below LRG $11.03/sq ft); M5 at $46.00 (exceeds small-format band ceiling).
- **Key Wave 1 finding:** price-to-area ratio ($44/$35 = 1.257) nearly exactly matches area ratio (3.202/2.56 = 1.251) — proportional pricing with a 0.5% fractional width premium.

**Wave 2 — Destruction Round (6 models, fresh chat, hostile attack vectors — run twice):**
- First run partially contaminated by nesting geometry misread by some models. Second run with explicit geometry clarification produced clean results.
- **Surviving Wave 2 findings:**
  1. $44.00 landing inside the small-format band is structurally superior to $43.25 landing below it — **the single most important finding**.
  2. 200+ tier requires a visible step-down, not flat compression.
  3. Fully-burdened margin at $44 is approximately 44–53% (below the 57% warn floor) — accepted as a strategic account concession.
  4. January 2027 normalization requires ~9% uplift from $44 anchor — manageable.
- **Wave 2 verdict:** Yes with modifications — raise to $44.00 anchor; add real 200+ compression; revise 200+ from $38 to $39 after the second run.

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn):**
- **All 6 models approved $44.00 at qty 20** with immediate PO same day. Zero pushback, zero question emails.
- Sean's mental normalization: $44 ÷ 3.202 = $13.74/sq ft — lands inside the small-format band, passes pattern-match in under 30 seconds.
- Pushback threshold: $47–$49 ($3–$5 of headroom above the anchor).
- 200+ at $38/$39 universally accepted — the visible $5–$6 step-down satisfies the volume reward expectation.
- Sean logs **$13.74/sq ft as a permanent Band A anchor** in the mental model; expects future 4.0 sq ft items at ~$54–$56.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- **6/6 UNANIMOUS YES** on $57 / $50 / $44 / $42 / $40 / $38 tier table.
- After the revised Wave 2, the 200+ tier was raised to $39 based on the fully-burdened margin analysis at high volume.
- No other modifications adopted.
- All open items resolved: geometry confirmed, material cost locked at $12.78, band coherence confirmed (Band A range $13.65–$13.94/sq ft holds at 3.202 sq ft), buyer simulation unanimous, §26 infrastructure handles all cliffs.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted Wave 4 unanimous consensus tier table.
- Raised 200+ from $38 to $39 based on revised Wave 2 fully-burdened margin analysis at high volume.
- No other modifications.
- **No override type — engine consensus accepted.** No relationship concession applied, no competitive defense applied, no owner judgment override applied.

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves (with a corrected Wave 2 second run for geometry clarity). Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES (6/6) on the tier table. No override.

**Profile Implication:** This item is the **first Band A data point outside the 2.51–2.56 sq ft small-format cluster**. It establishes the curve between the cluster and Band B (LRG at $11.03/sq ft), validating that Band A holds proportional pricing in the 3–5 sq ft range. Future Band A items at 1.0 sq ft ≤ sq ft < 5.0 sq ft validate against the expanded Band A (now 5 data points). Items near the Band B boundary (~4.5–5.5 sq ft) still require 4-wave AI validation before pricing.

| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $57.00 | $12.78 | $44.22 | ~77.6% |
| 10-19 | $50.00 | $12.78 | $37.22 | ~74.4% |
| 20-49 | $44.00 | $12.78 | $31.22 | ~70.9% |
| 50-99 | $42.00 | $12.78 | $29.22 | ~69.6% |
| 100-199 | $40.00 | $12.78 | $27.22 | ~68.1% |
| 200+ | $39.00 | $12.78 | $26.22 | ~67.2% |

**Margin at qty 20: ~70.9%.** Slightly below the cut vinyl category margin band (~73–78% target) on a material-only basis. The structural softness at this tier was accepted as a strategic account concession during Wave 2 — fully-burdened margin (~44–53%) at $44 is below the 57% warn floor and was visible to the engine; the concession was deliberately taken to land $44 inside the Band A $/sq ft range and trigger Sean's instant-approval pattern-match.

**Margin floor at 200+: ~67.2%.** Above the ~57% category warn threshold and well above the 50% structural stop. Acceptable for a Band A item with a deliberate concession at the anchor.

**Tier compression (1-9 → 200+):** 31.6% discount ($57 → $39). **Tighter** than Band A's cluster compression (~51%) — the 200+ tier is intentionally less aggressive because the LRG inversion floor ($35.32) constrains how low 200+ can go, and the fully-burdened margin analysis at high volume calls for a visible-but-modest step-down rather than a deep cut. The compression also aligns with the family position — a too-deep 200+ discount on MED would invert against the LRG band at high quantities.

**Comparison to Band A cluster (material-only margins at qty 20):**

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Material Cost | Margin (qty 20) |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| 1205720 (Cardinal Red, 24" roll) | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | $8.74 | ~75% |

The MED item sits just below the Band A material-only margin band at qty 20 (~71% vs cluster ~73–75%). The 3–4 percentage-point softness reflects the higher feed-length-driven material cost on a 49.16" cut (vs ~33.5" on the cluster) producing a higher material share of revenue at the same $/sq ft. The pricing-engine consensus held the band $/sq ft rather than chase material-cost-driven margin parity — the right call per Wave 4 unanimous verdict.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 3010704
- **Description:** LBL-ELLIOTT LRG RED — large-format "ELLIOTT" wordmark in Cardinal Red
- **Drawing:** 3010704.dwg
- **Revision:** B
- **Drawing Date:** 02/26/07 (Engineer: AEB / Drafter: DFL)
- **Type:** Vinyl cut lettering (no printing)
- **Content:** Block letter "ELLIOTT" wordmark — 7 characters, open counters (E ×3, O ×1). Generic brand wordmark used across all Elliott crane models — applied to crane body/boom for brand identification.
- **Dimensions:** 70-13/16" W × 14-3/8" H (70.8125" × 14.375" — **7.069 sq ft**)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" fractional (drawing note 1)
- **Status:** Quoted 2026-06-05

sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red
- **Drawing Callout:** "2-MILL, '3M 180-53 CARDINAL RED', 5-7 YEAR-VINYL, ADHESIVE BACK" (Elliott's "180-53" is shorthand for 180mC-53, consistent with all other Cardinal Red items on this account)
- **Film Type:** 2-mil cast vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Rated Durability:** 10-year (exceeds drawing spec of 5-7 year)
- **Roll Size:** **15" × 50yd** = $433.77/roll ($8.6754/yd, $2.3134/sq ft) — verified 2026-06-05 (see `materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red-15in.md`)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, **30" × 100yd** = $141.86/roll ($1.4186/yd, $0.1891/sq ft) — verified 2026-06-05 (see `materials/transferrite-582u-30in.md`)
- **Laminate:** N/A — cut vinyl, no laminate

> **Note on roll selection:** This item uses the 15" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (not the 24" × 50yd roll used for the 2.5 sq ft cluster). The 14.375" label height fits within the 15" roll width with 0.625" clearance, giving 1-across nesting. The 24" roll would waste 9.625" per row — operationally and economically wrong for this item. The 30" application tape similarly enables 2-up nesting (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" across 30" tape), which is required to amortize the tape feed across two labels.

sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Component | Roll | Cost | Cost/Yard | Cost/Sq Ft |
|-----------|------|------|-----------|------------|
| 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red | 15" × 50yd | $433.77 | $8.6754 | $2.3134 |
| TransferRite Ultra 582U | 30" × 100yd | $141.86 | $1.4186 | $0.1891 |

## Vinyl Nesting on 15" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across roll) | 14.375" |
| Labels across 15" roll | **1** (0.625" clearance) |
| Label length (down roll) | 70.8125" |
| Spacing allocation per label | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per label | 70.8125" + 6" = **76.8125" = 2.1337 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per label (length-based) | 2.1337 yd × $8.6754/yd = **$18.51** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 30" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across tape) | 14.375" |
| Labels across 30" tape | **2** (2 × 14.375" = 28.75", 1.25" waste strip) |
| Feed length per row of 2 | 76.8125" = 2.1337 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 2 | 2.1337 yd × $1.4186/yd = $3.03 |
| Tape cost per label | $3.03 ÷ 2 = **$1.51** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red, 15" × 50yd) | $18.51 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 30" × 100yd) | $1.51 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$20.02** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $20.02 ÷ 7.069 = $2.83/sq ft (vs $3.41/sq ft on the 2.5 sq ft benchmark — larger area amortizes the tape cost and improves per-sq-ft material economics).

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 15" Cardinal Red stock. 1 label across (14.375" height across 15" roll, 0.625" clearance). Single continuous cut path of the "ELLIOTT" wordmark.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 7 block characters. Large simple letters with open counters (E ×3, O ×1) — straightforward weeding, no fine interior detail.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 30" tape allows 2 labels side-by-side per masking pass (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" across 30" tape, 1.25" waste strip).
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 70.8125" length.
6. **Package** — flat or rolled (depending on logistics), masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application on crane body/boom.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (15" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width — well within tolerance for the 70.8125" feed length)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low–medium. 7 large block characters at large scale, open counters. Simpler geometry per sq ft than the "E190" / "E160" / "D115" model designations (which are similar-scale characters at smaller overall size). The increased physical scale slows weeding linearly with area, but the open-counter geometry keeps the weeding rate per inch comparable.
- **Cut feed length:** ~76.8" per label (label + 6" spacing). On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), ~23 labels per roll (1800 ÷ 76.8 = 23.4 → 23 complete labels with end waste).

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Sq ft scaling from the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster benchmark (P/N 1205720) with a 19.3% $/sq ft step-down to reflect the structural advantages of a large-format size class. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves).

**Sq ft comparison:**

| Item | Dimensions | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft |
|------|-----------|-------|----------------|---------|
| P/N 1205720 (small-format benchmark) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3017435 (small-format parity) | 43.91" × 8.38" | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 |
| P/N 3018378 (small-format parity) | 32.88" × 11.00" | 2.512 | $35 | $13.94 |
| P/N 1186310 (small-format parity) | 33-9/16" × 11" | 2.564 | $35 | $13.65 |
| **P/N 3010704 (large-format founding data point)** | **70.8125" × 14.375"** | **7.069** | **$78** | **$11.03** |

**Per sq ft at qty 20:** $78 ÷ 7.069 = **$11.03/sq ft**

**Step-down from small-format band:** $13.67/sq ft → $11.03/sq ft = **19.3% step-down**. Justified by three structural advantages of the large-format size class:
1. **Fixed labor amortization** — file import, machine setup, inspection, packaging are roughly constant per label regardless of physical size. The fixed-labor share of cost per sq ft drops as label area grows.
2. **Simpler weeding geometry per sq ft** — large block letters with open counters weed faster per sq ft than dense small-format text. The 7-character "ELLIOTT" wordmark with E ×3 and O ×1 open counters is geometrically simpler than a comparable area of small-format model designations.
3. **Lower material cost per sq ft** — $2.83/sq ft on this item vs $3.41/sq ft on the 2.5 sq ft benchmark. The 30" application tape amortizes 2-up against the 15" single-label vinyl feed, reducing tape cost per sq ft by ~$0.08/sq ft.

**Profile Validation:** Establishes the **Large-Format Cut Vinyl band (5+ sq ft)** in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at **$11.03/sq ft at qty 20**. This is a separate band from the existing 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession-phase band ($13.65–$13.94/sq ft). The two bands are independent — they do not contaminate or interact with each other. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster only, not to this large-format class.

## 4-Wave Validation Record (3010704)

> **NOTE:** Validation was conducted at 13.5" height (6.639 sq ft). Nick corrected the operative height to 14.375" (7.069 sq ft) after validation. Material cost is feed-length-driven, not area-driven — the vinyl cost ($18.51) depends on the 76.8125" feed length, which is unchanged; the tape cost ($1.51) depends on the same feed length and still nests 2-up on the 30" tape (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" ≤ 30"). All prices and margins are unchanged. $/sq ft figures were recalculated with the larger area (denominator change only).

**Wave 1 — Build (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Price range at qty 20: $63–$91. Central cluster: $75–$91. Median: ~$83.
- Fundamental debate: $/sq ft step-down at 2.6× the calibrated size.
- 2 models held band at $13.71/sq ft ($91). 4 models applied step-downs ($63–$85).
- All 6 confirmed $20.02 material cost. All 6 confirmed $0 file prep.

**Wave 2 — Destruction (6 models, hostile attack vectors):**
- Buyer/Procurement: 6/6 HIGH — flat $/sq ft across size classes is the structural error.
- Competitor: 6/6 HIGH — regional competitor at $55–$68 qty 20, $44–$50 qty 200+.
- Cost Auditor: 4/6 MEDIUM, 2/6 HIGH — 6" spacing padding ($0.40–$0.65), margin is material-only.
- Strategic: 6/6 HIGH — must establish separate large-format size class before first invoice.
- Verdicts: 6/6 rejected original table ($91 at qty 20).

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn):**
- All 6 pushed back. None approved as-is at $85.
- Pushback threshold range: $75–$85. Central cluster: $80–$82.
- Instant approval range: $65–$78. Central cluster: $78 (4 of 6).
- Incumbent estimate: $55–$70. All 6 referenced Pro Sign.
- PO decision: All 6 held PO. All 6 would send question/counteroffer email.
- All 6 confirmed this becomes a permanent data point in the buyer's pricing model.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- Verdict: **6/6 NO on $85 at qty 20.**
- All 6 recommended **$78 at qty 20. UNANIMOUS.**
- All 6 required separate large-format size class established before first invoice.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted $78 at qty 20 (unanimous Wave 4 recommendation).
- Set T1 at $105, T2 at $92 (operational tiers — Sean orders across T1–T3).
- Set T4–T6 at $68/$60/$52 for structural completeness.
- No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process.

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves. Unanimous on $78 at qty 20 in Wave 4. Unanimous on the requirement to establish a separate large-format size class before first invoice.

**Override:** None. Engine consensus accepted.

**Profile Implication:** This item establishes the founding data point for the Large-Format Cut Vinyl band (5+ sq ft) at $11.03/sq ft at qty 20. Future cut vinyl items at ≥ 5.0 sq ft validate against this band, NOT the 2.51–2.56 sq ft concession band.

- **FOUNDING DATA POINT — Large-Format Cut Vinyl size class (5+ sq ft).** This is the first item on the Elliott account at the large-format scale. Establishes the Large-Format Cut Vinyl band in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at **$11.03/sq ft at qty 20**.

The two bands are independent. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to the 2.51–2.56 sq ft cluster only, not to this large-format class.
- **4-wave AI validation (24 passes).** Pricing validated through the full 4-wave atomic AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS on $78 at qty 20 across all 6 models. No override type — engine consensus accepted.
- **Validation height vs operative height.** Validation was conducted using an initial height of 13.5" (6.639 sq ft). Nick subsequently corrected the operative height to 14.375" (7.069 sq ft). All prices and margins are unchanged because material cost is feed-length-driven, not area-driven. The vinyl cost ($18.51) depends on the 76.8125" feed length, which is unchanged. The tape cost ($1.51) depends on the same feed length and still nests 2-up on the 30" tape (2 × 14.375" = 28.75" ≤ 30"). The only figures that changed are sq ft (6.639 → 7.069), $/sq ft at each tier (recalculated with larger denominator), and the benchmark step-down percentage (14% → 19.3%). The $78 at qty 20 was validated unanimously by all 6 Wave 4 models.
- **Roll selection — 15" Cardinal Red and 30" TransferRite.** This item uses the **NEW** 15" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red-15in.md) at $433.77/roll and the **NEW** 30" × 100yd TransferRite 582U (materials/transferrite-582u-30in.md) at $141.86/roll. The 24" Cardinal Red roll used by the 2.5 sq ft cluster is wrong for this item — it would waste 9.625" per row. The 24" TransferRite 582U used by the 2.5 sq ft cluster is also wrong — it only fits 1 label per row at this height, doubling the tape cost per label. The 30" tape enables 2-up nesting, which is required to amortize the tape cost.
- **Material cost build is feed-length-driven.** Both vinyl and tape cost are calculated from feed length (76.8125" = 2.1337 yd per label), not from label area. This is the same length-based method used for the small-format cluster, but the larger feed length produces a larger absolute material cost ($20.02 vs $8.74).
- **No first article requested or offered.** Sean did not request FA, and Pro Label did not offer one. The item ships directly at production-tier pricing on whatever quantity is ordered.
- **No MOQ on any item on this account.**
- **Incumbent context (Wave 3 finding).** All 6 Wave 3 buyer-simulation models referenced Pro Sign as the likely incumbent estimate at $55–$70 at qty 20. The $78 quote is above incumbent but justified by Pro Label's track record on this account (15 prior items quoted, FA-accepted baseline, zero-drama fulfillment). The "instant approval" cluster was at $78 (4 of 6), which is what we sent.
- **Mental model risk (Wave 3 finding).** All 6 Wave 3 models confirmed that this price becomes a permanent data point in Sean's pricing model. He will normalize $/sq ft across cut vinyl items going forward and apply this band ($11.03/sq ft at qty 20) to any future large-format requests. The structural separation between the 2.5 sq ft band and this large-format band must be explicit in the quote email if Sean asks.
- **Drawing context.** Drawing dated 02/26/07, Revision B. This is a long-lived production item — Elliott has been making this generic ELLIOTT brand wordmark for ~19 years before Pro Label took over the program. Pro Sign (incumbent) made it for them previously.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 3010707
- **Description:** ElliottEquip.com URL Wordmark — Cardinal Red
- **Drawing:** D/3010707 (single drawing covers all three color variants)
- **Type:** Vinyl cut lettering (no printing)
- **Content:** "ElliottEquip.com" — single-line block letter wordmark, 16 mixed-case characters. Squared-off block character style with tight tracking. Brand identity URL applied to crane body or other equipment surface for marketing/contact purposes.
- **Dimensions:** 34.887" W × 4" H (**0.969 sq ft**)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" (assumed account default)
- **Status:** Quoted 2026-06-05

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red
- **Drawing/Email Callout:** "Cardinal Red 53" (Sean Finn email)
- **Film Type:** 2-mil cast vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Rated Durability:** 10-year (exceeds account default of 5-7 year)
- **Roll Size:** 24" × 50yd = $775.10/roll ($15.502/yd, $7.751/sq ft) — verified 2026-05-28 (see `materials/3m-180mc-cardinal-red.md`)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, 24" × 100yd = $118.21/roll ($1.1821/yd, $0.5911/sq ft) — verified 2026-05-28 (see `materials/transferrite-582u.md`)
- **Laminate:** N/A — cut vinyl, no laminate

> **Note on roll selection:** This item uses the 24" × 50yd Cardinal Red roll (the same roll used by the small-format Band A items 1205720 and 1186310). The 4" label height enables 5-across nesting on the 24" roll (5 × 4" = 20", 4" clearance), which is the optimal layout for this geometry. The 24" TransferRite 582U tape matches the vinyl roll width exactly — no overhang, no waste strip.

---
sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Component | Roll | Cost | Cost/Yard | Cost/Sq Ft |
|-----------|------|------|-----------|------------|
| 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red | 24" × 50yd | $775.10 | $15.502 | $7.751 |
| TransferRite Ultra 582U | 24" × 100yd | $118.21 | $1.1821 | $0.5911 |

## Vinyl Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across roll) | 4" |
| Labels across 24" roll | **5** (5 × 4" = 20", 4" clearance) |
| Label length (down roll) | 34.887" |
| Spacing allocation per row | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per row of 5 | 34.887" + 6" = **40.887" = 1.1357 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd × $15.502/yd = $17.603 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $17.603 ÷ 5 = **$3.52** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Tape width | 24" (matches vinyl roll width exactly) |
| Feed length per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.342 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.342 ÷ 5 = **$0.27** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red, 24" × 50yd) | $3.52 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 24" × 100yd) | $0.27 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$3.79** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $3.79 ÷ 0.969 = $3.91/sq ft.

## Labels per Roll

- Rows per 50yd roll: floor(50 / 1.1357) = **44 complete rows**
- Labels per roll: 44 × 5 = **220 labels per roll**

---
sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Cardinal Red stock. 5 labels across (5 × 4" = 20" across 24" roll, 4" clearance). Single continuous cut path for the "ElliottEquip.com" wordmark, repeated 5-up across the roll.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 16 mixed-case characters. Weeding considerations: 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots (period between "ElliottEquip" and "com", dot over lowercase "i"), tight descender channel on "q" adjacent to "u" stem. Each label requires careful weeding despite the small overall area.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 34.887" length, all enclosed counters cleanly removed, dots intact.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" Cardinal Red stock; 28" max cut width)
- **Weeding complexity:** Elevated relative to Band A model-designation items. 16 mixed-case characters at small scale, 4 enclosed counters, 2 isolated dots, tight q-u channel. Per-label weeding time is comparable to a Band A "E190" weeding pass despite the smaller sq ft, because the character count and enclosed-counter count are higher.
- **Cut feed length:** 40.887" per row of 5 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 44 complete rows × 5 labels = 220 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Founding data point for the **sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile. Validated through the full 4-wave AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` (24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves) plus a final independent industry-validation audit (6 models). 4-wave verdict was UNANIMOUS YES on $20 at qty 20.

**Sq ft / $/sq ft comparison across the three cut vinyl bands:**

| Band | Representative Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/sq ft | Step from previous |
|------|---------------------|-------|----------------|---------|--------------------|
| Band B (Large-Format) | 3010704 | 7.069 | $78 | $11.03 | — |
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1205720 | 2.56 | $35 | $13.67 | +24% |
| **Band C (Sub-1 sq ft, new)** | **3010707 (this item)** | **0.969** | **$20** | **$20.64** | **+51%** |

Monotonically increasing $/sq ft as sq ft drops. No tier-level inversion at any quantity.

**Band C justification — 51% $/sq ft step-up from Band A:** Three structural drivers:

1. **Fixed-labor dominance on sub-1 sq ft items.** Setup, weeding, masking, inspection, and packaging take approximately the same time per label regardless of size. At 0.969 sq ft (2.64× smaller than Band A's 2.56 sq ft), these fixed costs amortize over much less area. Fixed-labor share of $/sq ft is structurally higher on sub-1 sq ft items.
2. **Elevated weeding complexity per sq ft.** "ElliottEquip.com" is 16 mixed-case characters with 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots, and a tight descender channel (q adjacent to u stem). Band A's "E190" / "E160" / "D115" / "ELLIOTT" designations are 3-7 uppercase block characters with open geometry — much faster weeding per sq ft.
3. **Higher material cost per sq ft.** $3.91/sq ft (Cardinal Red, worst-case) vs ~$3.41/sq ft on Band A. The 5-across nesting on 24" roll is efficient, but the smaller label area produces a higher $/sq ft material rate.

**Material cost build at Cardinal Red (worst-case anchor):**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (24" × 50yd Cardinal Red, $15.502/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $15.502/yd | $3.52 |
| Application tape (24" × 100yd TransferRite 582U, $1.1821/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $1.1821/yd | $0.27 |
| **Total per label** | | **$3.79** |

## 4-Wave Validation Record

**Wave 1 — Build (6 models, independent, atomic prompts):**
- Price range at qty 20: $16.50–$34.00. Three distinct clusters identified.
  - **Low cluster ($16.50–$19.00):** Proportional $/sq ft scaling from Band A. Models held the Band A $/sq ft rate and scaled by area only.
  - **Mid cluster ($22.00):** Power-law $/sq ft + complexity premium. Single model used a fitted curve from Bands A and B.
  - **High cluster ($31.00–$34.00):** Absolute labor recovery. Models built up from fixed minutes per label × shop rate, ignoring size relative to Band A.
- All 6 confirmed: $/sq ft must exceed Band A; fixed labor dominant at this size class; weeding complexity premium justified; monotonic $/sq ft curve required across all three bands; Cardinal Red worst-case anchor approach correct.
- All 6 confirmed $3.79 material cost (Cardinal Red worst case). All 6 confirmed $0 file prep.

**Wave 2 — Destruction (6 models, hostile attack vectors):**
- **Buyer/Procurement: H (6/6).** High cluster destroyed. $31–$34 triggers competitive bidding from Sean — he can pattern-match against the Band A $35/qty 20 (3.7× the sq ft, same price) and conclude Pro Label is gouging on small items.
- **Competitor: H (6/6).** Regional sign shop quotes for "ElliottEquip.com" at this geometry land at $13–$18 at qty 20 (incumbent Pro Sign reference). High cluster is uncompetitive.
- **Cost Auditor: M (6/6).** True material+labor cost ~$6–$9/label including weeding labor at this complexity. Low cluster margins defensible at ~60-65% true margin. Mid cluster healthy. High cluster has "air" beyond shop-rate justification.
- **Strategic: H (6/6).** High cluster creates an unsustainable precedent at the January 2027 Band A normalization. If Band A moves from $13.67/sq ft to $15–$16/sq ft, sub-1 sq ft items priced at $32/sq ft compress the band ratios in a way that becomes hard to defend.
- **Verdict: 5 No / 1 Yes-with-mods.** Convergence to $18–$22 at qty 20. Central tendency: $20.

**Wave 3 — Buyer Simulation (6 models as Sean Finn, Employee-Owner, $140K/year program, $/sq ft mental model):**
- All 6 approved the PO at $20/qty 20.
  - 5 of 6: as-is, no question.
  - 1 of 6: with a non-blocking parallel question ("how does this scale if we wanted to do this in larger formats?" — informational, not pricing).
- Pushback threshold: $22–$24/sq ft. Below that, Sean clears it without thinking.
- Instant approval: $18/sq ft (unanimous). $20.64/sq ft is just above the instant-approval line but well below the pushback line.
- All 6 logged **$20.64/sq ft as a permanent sub-1 sq ft anchor in their mental model.** Future quotes above this level will be challenged.

**Wave 4 — Final Synthesis (6 models, binary verdicts):**
- **6/6 YES on the full tier table $28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.** UNANIMOUS.
- 4 of 6: send as-is, no modifications.
- 1 of 6: suggested raising 50-99 from $16.50 → $17.50 (not adopted — small change, no defense need).
- 1 of 6: suggested raising 200+ from $11.50 → $12.50 (not adopted — Sean's projected volumes don't reach 200+ frequently; no margin concern).
- No model rejected any tier. No model called for a $/sq ft change at qty 20.
- All 6 confirmed: $20.64/sq ft survives the January 2027 Band A normalization. When Band A moves to $15–$16/sq ft, the Band C premium compresses from 51% to 26–39% — **structurally better**, not worse.

## Final Industry Validation (6 models, independent audit)

After the 4-wave AI validation, an additional 6-model independent industry-validation audit was run against the locked tier table.

- **6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH.**
- $20.64/sq ft at qty 20 placed at the upper-middle of the specialty shop range for sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl ($17–$25/sq ft).
- Tier structure sound: 59% compression top-to-bottom, 14–18% step-downs at each tier. Within standard cut vinyl tier compression conventions.
- $/sq ft curve validated: 51% step-up Band A → Band C falls within expected 45–60% range for a 2.6× sq ft compression at this character/complexity profile.
- Margins appropriate for a specialty 2-person shop: 67–86.5% across tiers. Above the 50% structural floor at every tier including 200+.
- Multi-color identical pricing (3010707/3010708/3010709 all at the same tier table) confirmed as universal industry standard for color-variant runs of identical geometry.
- No structural risk to the $140K Elliott account from this price point.

**Nick's Final Decision:**
- Accepted the Wave 2/3/4 consensus tier table: **$28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.**
- No modifications adopted from the two Wave 4 suggestions (both were soft "could" suggestions, not "must" findings).
- All three colors (3010707 Cardinal Red, 3010708 Black, 3010709 White) carry the identical tier table. Material cost differs by color; margin differs by color; price does NOT differ by color. Standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry. Cardinal Red is the worst-case material anchor; pricing decisions use the Cardinal Red figure.
- **No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process and Nick accepted the consensus.**

**AI Validation Summary:** 24 independent responses across 6 models × 4 waves. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES on the tier table. Final industry validation: 6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH. Pricing locked.

**Profile Implication:** This item, together with 3010708 (Black) and 3010709 (White), establishes the **founding data point cluster for the sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** at **$20.64/sq ft at qty 20**. Band C is INDEPENDENT of Bands A and B — the three bands do not contaminate or interact. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to Band A only, not Band C.

---
sections.margin_analysis:
| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost (Red) | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $28.00 | $3.79 | $24.21 | ~86.5% |
| 10-19 | $24.00 | $3.79 | $20.21 | ~84.2% |
| 20-49 | $20.00 | $3.79 | $16.21 | ~81.1% |
| 50-99 | $16.50 | $3.79 | $12.71 | ~77.0% |
| 100-199 | $13.50 | $3.79 | $9.71 | ~71.9% |
| 200+ | $11.50 | $3.79 | $7.71 | ~67.0% |

**Margin at qty 20: ~81.1%.** Above the cut vinyl category margin band (~73–78% target) — the small-format premium produces a structurally higher margin floor on the Cardinal Red (worst-case) variant.

**Margin floor at 200+: ~67%.** Well above the ~57% category warn threshold and far above the 50% stop threshold. Sub-1 sq ft items inherently support a higher margin floor than Band A because the per-unit material cost is small relative to the price.

**Tier compression (1-9 → 200+):** ~59% discount ($28 → $11.50). Slightly more compression than Band A's 51% — tighter tier ratios reflect the larger relative price drops appropriate to higher-volume orders at this size class.

**Comparison to category band:**

| Band | Representative Item | Margin at qty 20 (anchor color) | Margin floor at 200+ |
|------|---------------------|----------------------------------|----------------------|
| Band A (Small-Format) | 1205720 (Cardinal Red) | ~75% | ~60% |
| Band B (Large-Format) | 3010704 (Cardinal Red) | ~74% | ~61.5% |
| **Band C (Sub-1 sq ft)** | **3010707 (Cardinal Red, worst case)** | **~81%** | **~67%** |

Margin profile is structurally higher on Band C because the material-cost share of revenue is smaller at this size class — the same fixed-labor amortization that drives the higher $/sq ft also drives the higher margin floor.

**Color-specific margin reference (P/N 3010707 alone is Cardinal Red; the other two colors are documented for cross-reference visibility):**

| P/N | Color | Material Cost | Margin at qty 20 |
|-----|-------|---------------|-------------------|
| **3010707** | **Cardinal Red (worst case)** | **$3.79** | **~81.1%** |
| 3010708 | Black | $3.26 | ~83.7% |
| 3010709 | White | $3.08 | ~84.6% |

---
sections.notes:
- **FOUNDING DATA POINT — Sub-1 sq ft Cut Vinyl band (Band C).** Together with P/N 3010708 (Black) and P/N 3010709 (White), this item establishes the sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band in the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at **$20.64/sq ft at qty 20**.
- **Pricing anchor item for Band C.** P/N 3010707 (Cardinal Red) is the canonical pricing anchor because Cardinal Red is the worst-case material cost ($3.79/label). All margin reasoning in this band uses the Cardinal Red figure. The other two colors (3010708 Black, 3010709 White) share the identical tier table and have better margins.
- **Three INDEPENDENT P/Ns, identical pricing.** P/N 3010707 (Cardinal Red), 3010708 (Black), and 3010709 (White) share identical dimensions, identical process, and identical pricing. They are NOT a kit or set — each is ordered independently by Sean at whatever quantity he needs. Drawing D/3010707 covers all three items. Color-only difference. Identical pricing across colors is standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry, confirmed by the final 6-model industry-validation audit.
- **Separate from Bands A and B.** Band C is an INDEPENDENT band — it does not contaminate or interact with Band A (small-format, 2.51–2.56 sq ft) or Band B (large-format, 5+ sq ft). The three bands have different anchors, different $/sq ft, different material cost ratios. The January 2027 normalization plan applies to Band A only, NOT to Band C.
- **4-wave AI validation (24 passes) + final industry audit (6 models).** Pricing validated through the full 4-wave atomic AI validation process per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. Wave 4 was UNANIMOUS YES on the full tier table across all 6 models. Final independent industry audit: 6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH. No override type — engine consensus accepted.
- **51% $/sq ft step-up from Band A is structurally warranted, not a concession reversal.** The step-up reflects fixed-labor dominance on sub-1 sq ft items + elevated weeding complexity (16 mixed-case characters, 4 enclosed counters, 2 dots, tight q-u channel) + higher material cost per sq ft. This is a SIZE-CLASS adjustment validated unanimously by all 6 Wave 4 models, not a relationship/concession adjustment.
- **Survives January 2027 Band A normalization.** All 6 Wave 4 models confirmed that when Band A moves from $13.67/sq ft to $15–$16/sq ft, the Band C premium compresses from 51% to 26–39% — structurally better, not worse. The Band C tier table does not need to move at the January 2027 inflection.
- **Mental model risk (Wave 3 finding).** All 6 Wave 3 buyer-simulation models confirmed that this price ($20.64/sq ft at qty 20) becomes a permanent data point in Sean's pricing model. He will normalize $/sq ft across sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl items going forward and apply this band to any future sub-1 sq ft requests. Pushback threshold is $22–$24/sq ft; instant approval below $18/sq ft. The $20.64/sq ft locked in this session sits between those two limits.
- **No first article requested or offered.** Sean did not request FA, and Pro Label did not offer one. The items ship directly at production-tier pricing on whatever quantity is ordered. Same posture as 3010704.
- **No MOQ on any item on this account.**
- **Quote language — multi-color framing.** Sean confirmed via email that 3010707, 3010708, and 3010709 are three independent P/Ns sharing drawing D/3010707 with only color differing. The quote email should list all three P/Ns with the same tier table and a single material/process description, with a one-line note that each P/N can be ordered independently.

---

sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Color-parity pricing with the Band C founding data point (P/N 3010707, Cardinal Red). Identical dimensions, identical process, identical tier table. Color is the only variable; price does not differ by color. Standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry.

**Benchmark item:** P/N 3010707 (Cardinal Red) — the canonical pricing anchor for Band C. The 4-wave AI validation record and final industry validation are documented in full in `items/3010707.md`. P/N 3010707 carries the worst-case material cost ($3.79/label) and is the basis for all margin reasoning in this band.

**Why identical pricing across colors:** All three P/Ns (3010707 Cardinal Red, 3010708 Black, 3010709 White) cover the same drawing (D/3010707), the same dimensions (34.887" × 4" = 0.969 sq ft), the same cut/weed/mask process, the same weeding complexity (16 mixed-case characters, 4 enclosed counters, 2 dots, tight q-u channel), and the same nesting layout (5-up on 24" × 50yd roll). Material cost varies by color, but the labor profile, the nesting profile, and the buyer's $/sq ft mental model do not. Differential pricing by color on identical geometry would create a procurement-side problem and would not survive the Wave 3 buyer-simulation test (Sean expects identical prices for color-only differences on the same drawing).

**4-Wave AI Validation Summary** (full record in `items/3010707.md`):

| Wave | Outcome |
|------|---------|
| Wave 1 (Build) | $16.50–$34.00 at qty 20. Three clusters (Low/Mid/High). All 6 confirmed $/sq ft must exceed Band A; fixed labor dominant; weeding complexity premium justified; Cardinal Red worst-case anchor correct. |
| Wave 2 (Destruction) | High cluster destroyed by Buyer/Competitor/Strategic attacks. Cost Auditor moderate concerns on low cluster margins. **Convergence to $18–$22 at qty 20, central $20.** Verdict: 5 No / 1 Yes-with-mods on original tables. |
| Wave 3 (Buyer Sim) | All 6 approved PO at $20/qty 20 (5 as-is, 1 with non-blocking question). Pushback threshold $22–$24/sq ft; instant approval $18/sq ft. All 6 logged $20.64/sq ft as permanent sub-1 sq ft anchor. |

sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Component | Roll | Cost | Cost/Yard | Cost/Sq Ft |
|-----------|------|------|-----------|------------|
| 3M Controltac 180mC-10 White | 24" × 50yd | $619.21 | $12.3842 | $6.1921 |
| TransferRite Ultra 582U | 24" × 100yd | $118.21 | $1.1821 | $0.5911 |

## Vinyl Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label height (across roll) | 4" |
| Labels across 24" roll | **5** (5 × 4" = 20", 4" clearance) |
| Label length (down roll) | 34.887" |
| Spacing allocation per row | 3" lead-in + 3" trail-out = 6" |
| Feed length per row of 5 | 34.887" + 6" = **40.887" = 1.1357 yd** |
| Vinyl cost per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd × $12.3842/yd = $14.063 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $14.063 ÷ 5 = **$2.81** |

## Application Tape Nesting on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Tape width | 24" (matches vinyl roll width exactly) |
| Feed length per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd (same as vinyl) |
| Tape cost per row of 5 | 1.1357 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.342 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.342 ÷ 5 = **$0.27** |

## Total Material Cost per Label

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-10 White, 24" × 50yd) | $2.81 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U, 24" × 100yd) | $0.27 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$3.08** |

**Material cost per sq ft:** $3.08 ÷ 0.969 = $3.18/sq ft.

## Labels per Roll

- Rows per 50yd roll: floor(50 / 1.1357) = **44 complete rows**
- Labels per roll: 44 × 5 = **220 labels per roll**

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" White stock. 5 labels across (5 × 4" = 20" across 24" roll, 4" clearance). Single continuous cut path for the "ElliottEquip.com" wordmark, repeated 5-up across the roll.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around the 16 mixed-case characters. Weeding considerations: 4 enclosed counters (e ×2, o ×1, p ×1), 2 isolated dots (period between "ElliottEquip" and "com", dot over lowercase "i"), tight descender channel on "q" adjacent to "u" stem. Each label requires careful weeding despite the small overall area.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. 24" tape matches 24" vinyl exactly.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, no lifted edges, character legibility across the 34.887" length, all enclosed counters cleanly removed, dots intact.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier, ready for hand-application.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" White stock; 28" max cut width)
- **Weeding complexity:** Elevated relative to Band A model-designation items. 16 mixed-case characters at small scale, 4 enclosed counters, 2 isolated dots, tight q-u channel. Per-label weeding time is comparable to a Band A "E190" weeding pass despite the smaller sq ft, because the character count and enclosed-counter count are higher.
- **Cut feed length:** 40.887" per row of 5 labels. On the 50yd roll (1800" usable), 44 complete rows × 5 labels = 220 labels per roll.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
**Methodology:** Color-parity pricing with the Band C founding data point (P/N 3010707, Cardinal Red). Identical dimensions, identical process, identical tier table. Color is the only variable; price does not differ by color. Standard industry practice for multi-color variants of identical geometry.

**Benchmark item:** P/N 3010707 (Cardinal Red) — the canonical pricing anchor for Band C. The 4-wave AI validation record and final industry validation are documented in full in `items/3010707.md`. P/N 3010707 carries the worst-case material cost ($3.79/label) and is the basis for all margin reasoning in this band.

**Why identical pricing across colors:** All three P/Ns (3010707 Cardinal Red, 3010708 Black, 3010709 White) cover the same drawing (D/3010707), the same dimensions (34.887" × 4" = 0.969 sq ft), the same cut/weed/mask process, the same weeding complexity (16 mixed-case characters, 4 enclosed counters, 2 dots, tight q-u channel), and the same nesting layout (5-up on 24" × 50yd roll). Material cost varies by color, but the labor profile, the nesting profile, and the buyer's $/sq ft mental model do not. Differential pricing by color on identical geometry would create a procurement-side problem and would not survive the Wave 3 buyer-simulation test (Sean expects identical prices for color-only differences on the same drawing).

**4-Wave AI Validation Summary** (full record in `items/3010707.md`):

| Wave | Outcome |
|------|---------|
| Wave 1 (Build) | $16.50–$34.00 at qty 20. Three clusters (Low/Mid/High). All 6 confirmed $/sq ft must exceed Band A; fixed labor dominant; weeding complexity premium justified; Cardinal Red worst-case anchor correct. |
| Wave 2 (Destruction) | High cluster destroyed by Buyer/Competitor/Strategic attacks. Cost Auditor moderate concerns on low cluster margins. **Convergence to $18–$22 at qty 20, central $20.** Verdict: 5 No / 1 Yes-with-mods on original tables. |
| Wave 3 (Buyer Sim) | All 6 approved PO at $20/qty 20 (5 as-is, 1 with non-blocking question). Pushback threshold $22–$24/sq ft; instant approval $18/sq ft. All 6 logged $20.64/sq ft as permanent sub-1 sq ft anchor. |
| Wave 4 (Final Synthesis) | **6/6 UNANIMOUS YES on the full tier table $28 / $24 / $20 / $16.50 / $13.50 / $11.50.** No tier rejected. All 6 confirmed $20.64/sq ft survives Jan 2027 Band A normalization (premium compresses 51% → 26–39%, structurally better). |

**Final industry validation:** 6/6 APPROVE, confidence HIGH. Multi-color identical pricing (3010707/3010708/3010709) confirmed as universal industry standard for color-variant runs of identical geometry.

**Material cost on this item (White):**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Vinyl (24" × 50yd White, $12.3842/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $12.3842/yd | $2.81 |
| Application tape (24" × 100yd TransferRite 582U, $1.1821/yd) | 1.1357 yd ÷ 5 labels/row × $1.1821/yd | $0.27 |
| **Total per label** | | **$3.08** |

**Margin on this item is structurally the best in the Band C cluster.** White material at $3.08/label vs Cardinal Red at $3.79/label produces ~3.5 points higher margin at every tier. White is the lowest-cost color in the band.

**Nick's Final Decision:** Accepted color-parity pricing — identical tier table to P/N 3010707. No override type — pricing validated through full 4-wave process on the band's anchor item.

**Profile Implication:** Founding data point cluster for the **sub-1 sq ft cut vinyl band (Band C)** at **$20.64/sq ft at qty 20**, together with 3010707 and 3010708. Band C is INDEPENDENT of Bands A and B.

sections.nesting:
### On 24" Roll
- 2 labels per row (8.38" + 8.38" = 16.76", fits 24" width)
- Linear footage per row: ~43.91" (~1.22 yards)
- Vinyl cost per label: ~$8.00 (1.22 yards × $13.116/yard ÷ 2) — unchanged
- Tape (length-based): feed 43.91" + 6" spacing = 49.91" = 1.3864 yd; 1.3864 × $1.1821/yd ÷ 2 labels/row = **$0.819/label**
- **Total: $8.00 + $0.819 = $8.82/label**

### On 48" Roll (dramatically better yield)
- 5 labels across (8.38" × 5 = 41.9", fits 48" width)
- Linear footage per row: ~43.91" (~1.22 yards)
- Vinyl cost per label: ~$6.28 (1.22 yards × $25.744/yard ÷ 5) — unchanged
- Tape (length-based): same 1.3864 yd feed ÷ 5 labels/row = 1.3864 × $1.1821 ÷ 5 = **$0.328/label**
- **Total: $6.281 + $0.328 = $6.61/label**

> **Tape cost corrected 2026-06-09** from area method (pseudo-rate: 2.56 sq ft × $0.5911/sq ft = $1.513) to the length-based method for consistency with Band B, Band C, and 3010701. Tape amortizes across the labels per row, same as vinyl. No sell price change.

### Recommendation
48" roll for any order of qty 10+. The per-label savings ($2.21) justify the larger roll purchase. 24" roll for small or one-off orders where stocking 48" isn't warranted. Frontmatter `material_cost_per_unit` files the conservative 24" roll cost ($8.82).

sections.pricing_derivation:
- **Methodology:** Material-proportional scaling from the Orajet printed/laminated singles band. This is the FOUNDING item for the Convex High Bond + Polycarbonate material family — no prior data point exists in this family. The price was derived through internal methodology, not external market benchmarking.

- **Derivation steps:**
  1. Interpolated the Orajet singles band $/sq ft at 1.102 sq ft (~$15.48/sq ft at qty 20)
  2. Calculated the material cost ratio: Convex+PC combined $/sq ft ÷ Orajet+polyester combined $/sq ft
     - Variant B: $3.5081 ÷ $1.9489 = **1.800×**
     - Variant A: $3.0576 ÷ $1.9489 = **1.569×**
  3. Scaled: $15.48 × ratio = target $/sq ft, multiplied by 1.102 sq ft area, snapped to $0.25 increments (§30)
     - Variant B: $15.48 × 1.800 = $27.86 → $27.90/sq ft at price of **$30.75**
     - Variant A: $15.48 × 1.569 = $24.29 → $24.27/sq ft at price of **$26.75**
  4. Built tiers using the standard account tier ratios (1-9 at ~1.50×, 10-19 at ~1.20×, etc.)
  5. Verified 87% margin at qty 20 — identical to every Orajet printed/laminated item

- **Comparable Items:**
  - **1230820** (1.296 sq ft, Orajet/polyester, $20.00 at qty 20, $15.43/sq ft, FA Accepted) — Root benchmark. Closest by size. Same process. The $/sq ft ratio between 3017557 Variant B and 1230820 equals the material cost ratio (1.80×), confirming proportional scaling.
  - **1278980** (0.609 sq ft, Orajet/polyester, $9.50 at qty 20, $15.60/sq ft) — Nearest validated singles-band data point by process equivalence.

- **Validation:**
  - No multi-round AI validation was run on the final locked price — pricing was locked by Nick.
  - Extensive external market research (3 rounds of Gemini deep research, 6 models per round) confirmed the market band for vinyl-base + polycarbonate-overlaminate overlays at qty 10–25 is $40–$70/sq ft. The recommended price sits ~30% below the market floor, consistent with the ~25–30% relationship concession on the Orajet band.
  - A full Claude Code methodology audit (Session AD) independently derived $30.75 by applying the account's own pricing rules, band structure, and margin targets to the new material family.
  - Prior external validation waves (dozens of AI model rounds) produced prices of $44–$57 at qty 20. These were rejected because every external model benchmarked against the rigid polycarbonate industrial nameplate/overlay market — the wrong reference class. P/N 3017557 is a printed vinyl label with a polycarbonate overlaminate, produced on the same equipment with the same process as every Orajet item on the account.

- **Override:** Strategic Anchor — this price establishes the founding $/sq ft band for the entire Convex/polycarbonate material family. Every future item in this family scales from this data point.

- **Profile Validation:** Establishes the Convex High Bond + Polycarbonate Pricing Profile. Variant B at $27.90/sq ft, Variant A at $24.27/sq ft at qty 20.

---
sections.notes:
- **FOUNDING DATA POINT.** First item in the Convex High Bond + Polycarbonate material family. Establishes the $/sq ft band for the entire family. Every future Convex/PC item scales from this data point.
- **Strategic Anchor override.** The pricing was derived from the internal material-proportional scaling methodology, not from external market data. External market data ($40–$70/sq ft) confirms the price is ~30% below market — a relationship concession, consistent with the Orajet band.
- **Two variants on one P/N.** Both 5-mil and 10-mil polycarbonate variants are quoted. Variant B (10-mil Kapco) is the primary — in-stock material, higher spec. Frontmatter reflects Variant B pricing. Variant A pricing documented in the Pricing and Margin sections.
- **Not ANSI.** This is a control panel overlay, not an ANSI safety label. §29 (ANSI account rule for Orajet/lam items) does NOT extend to the Convex/PC family.

- **First article:** $75.00, consistent with 1205720 FA price from April.
- **File prep:** $0 per account rule §22 — Elliott provides production-ready DWG files.
- **Application:** Safety-critical. Primary operator control interface on basket control box of an industrial aerial work platform. Equipment value $100,000+.

Both dimensions fit the laminator's 13.5" maximum width capacity independently (7.0" = 6.5" clearance; 7.5" = 6.0" clearance). Orientation is therefore a nesting-efficiency choice, not a forced constraint.

Label: 7.5" × 7.0". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28". Orienting the 7.0" dimension across the print bed nests 4 labels per row with zero waste (4 × 7.0" = 28.0"); orienting the 7.5" dimension across the bed nests only 3 per row with 5.5" waste (3 × 7.5" = 22.5"). The 7.0"-across orientation is the more efficient nesting choice and is used for production.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves) run in Claude Chat, followed by a Nick-directed One-Time Exception override on the two deepest tiers. Claude Code did not re-derive or second-guess the validated 1-9 through 50-99 tiers; the 100-199/200+ override is Nick's explicit final-number decision, documented here per Core Rule 12.**

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 3017572 LBL - HYDAC VLV OVERRIDE. 7.5" × 7.0" = 0.364583 sq ft (filed 0.365). Single label, 1 piece. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. ANSI by §29 account rule. 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope routing (Decision Tree Step 1a from `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`):**

| Threshold | Value | Result |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| sq ft < 0.1? | 0.365 ≥ 0.1 | NOT Micro-Format Band |
| 0.1 ≤ sq ft < 0.5? | 0.365 in range | **SUB-SCOPE HANDLING** |
| sq ft ≥ 0.5? | 0.365 < 0.5 | NOT Singles Band |

**Routing: Sub-scope (0.1-0.5 sq ft).**

---

## Step 2 — Governing Comparables

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft (qty 20) | Notes |
|------|-------|------------------|--------------------|-------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark / hard floor) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root — hard floor per §31 |
| 3024595 (governing sub-scope comparable) | 0.488 | $7.75 | $15.88 | Largest sub-scope item on file, fully §31-compliant |
| **3017572 (this item)** | **0.365** | **$6.00** | **$16.44** | Sits between 1210810 and 3024595 on the sub-scope gradient |
| 1210810 (nearest sub-scope comparable below) | 0.292 | $4.75 | $16.27 | Sub-scope; grandfathered upper tiers (pre-§31) |

3017572 prices ABOVE 3024595 on a $/sq ft basis ($16.44 vs $15.88), correctly reflecting its smaller area (25% smaller) per the normal size-cost relationship. This corrects an inversion the original flat-from-20-49 candidate would have created (see Step 4, Wave 2).

---

## Step 3 — Material Cost (Computed Fresh This Session)

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.365 × $1.21 | $0.4417 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.365 × $0.2389 | $0.0872 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 0.365 × $0.50 | $0.1825 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.7114** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) | judgment-applied | + $0.0386 |
| **Canonical material cost** | | **$0.75** |

**$/sq ft at qty 20:** $6.00 / 0.365 = **$16.44/sq ft** — above the $15.43/sq ft root floor (see Step 5).

---

## Step 4 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary (Already Complete — Recorded, Not Re-Derived)

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation, 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 independent responses, run in Claude Chat per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`.

**Wave 1 (Build):** Unanimous 6/6 on the §31 floor ($5.75) and flat termination from at least 50-99 through 200+. Split 4/6 (flat $5.75 from 20-49 onward) vs 2/6 (one step at $6.00 for 20-49, flat from 50-99). No kill criteria triggered by any model.

**Wave 2 (Destruction):** Unanimous 6/6 rejection of the flat-from-20-49 structure. Reason: it priced this smaller label (0.365 sq ft) at a LOWER $/sq ft ($15.75) than the larger 3024595 (0.488 sq ft, $15.88/sq ft) — an inversion of the normal size-cost relationship (smaller labels carry proportionally higher setup/handling cost per sq ft). Unanimous specific fix: raise 20-49 to $6.00, hold 50-99/100-199/200+ flat at $5.75.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation):** Split verdict. 3/6 models: Sean questions the $6.00 20-49 price and asks for ~$5.75-$5.80, citing the 3024595 comp. 3/6 models: Sean approves without friction, reading the 3.5% premium over 3024595 as proportionate to the 25% smaller area. Universal instant-approval number: $5.75-$5.85. Universal finding: landing exactly at $5.75 (the floor) at Sean's real qty-20 order volume would let him permanently defend $15.75/sq ft as the class price — worse long-term than a contested $6.00.

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis):** 5/6 YES — ship $6.00 at 20-49 as-is; the long-term protection of the size-price curve against 3024595 outweighs the ~50% chance of one clarifying, easily-answered email. 1/6 NO — recommended pulling to $5.80 to eliminate all friction. Consensus recommendation for 100-199 and 200+: $5.75 (matching 50-99, per the §31 floor doctrine established on 3024595) — no model in any wave proposed pricing below $5.75 at any tier.

**Nick's decision:** Accepted the Wave 4 majority for 1-9 through 50-99 ($8.75 / $7.25 / $6.00 / $5.75). Then directed a separate One-Time Exception override lowering 100-199 to $5.50 and 200+ to $5.25 — both below the validated $5.75 recommendation and below the §31 floor. See Step 7.

---

## Step 5 — Floor Doctrine Verification

| Tier | Price | $/Sq Ft | Above $15.43/sq ft Floor? |
|------|-------|---------|-----------------------------|
| 1-9 | $8.75 | $23.97 | ✓ Yes (+55.3%) |
| 10-19 | $7.25 | $19.86 | ✓ Yes (+28.7%) |
| 20-49 | $6.00 | $16.44 | ✓ Yes (+6.5%) |
| 50-99 | $5.75 | $15.75 | ✓ Yes (+2.1%) |
| 100-199 | $5.50 | $15.07 | **✗ No (-2.3%) — One-Time Exception** |
| 200+ | $5.25 | $14.38 | **✗ No (-6.8%) — One-Time Exception** |

**The 1-9 through 50-99 tiers all clear the §31 floor.** The 100-199 and 200+ tiers do not — see Step 7 for the override documentation.

---

## Step 6 — §26 Invoice Protection Check

| Boundary | Smaller Qty Total | Larger Qty Total | Cliff | Resolution |
|----------|---------------------|---------------------|-------|-----------|
| 9/10 | 9 × $8.75 = $78.75 | 10 × $7.25 = $72.50 | $6.25 | §26: buyer charged $72.50 for qty 9 |
| 19/20 | 19 × $7.25 = $137.75 | 20 × $6.00 = $120.00 | $17.75 | §26: buyer charged $120.00 for qty 19 |
| 49/50 | 49 × $6.00 = $294.00 | 50 × $5.75 = $287.50 | $6.50 | §26: buyer charged $287.50 for qty 49 |
| 99/100 | 99 × $5.75 = $569.25 | 100 × $5.50 = $550.00 | $19.25 | §26: buyer charged $550.00 for qty 99 |
| 199/200 | 199 × $5.50 = $1,094.50 | 200 × $5.25 = $1,050.00 | $44.50 | §26: buyer charged $1,050.00 for qty 199 |

All boundaries resolve cleanly under §26 — no ladder inversions. All cliffs are standard tier-step artifacts, handled at billing per account convention.

---

## Step 7 — One-Time Exception Override: 100-199 and 200+ Below the §31 Floor

**Engine's original (validated) recommendation:** $5.75 for both 100-199 and 200+, matching the 50-99 tier — fully §31-compliant, unanimous across all 4 waves and 24 model responses. No model at any wave proposed a price below $5.75 for any tier.

**Nick's final number:** $5.50 for 100-199 ($15.07/sq ft, $0.36/sq ft below the $15.43 floor) and $5.25 for 200+ ($14.38/sq ft, $1.05/sq ft below the floor).

**Override type:** One-Time Exception (Core Rule 12/13 — non-precedent-setting). This override was made after the validation record closed and reflects Nick's judgment on this specific quote, not a re-litigation of the §31 doctrine established on 3024595.

**Governance implication:** Per Core Rule 14, any future item that might look to 3017572 as a benchmark for its 100-199/200+ tiers must instead use the engine's original recommendation ($5.75, $15.75/sq ft) as the reference point — not the overridden $5.50/$5.25 figures. The §31 doctrine remains fully in force for all other items and for this item's own 1-9 through 50-99 tiers.

---

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **⚠ ONE-OFF ORDER. NOT A CATALOG PRICE. NOT A BENCHMARK.** Qty 6 for a unit in the field. The $9.17/label number is a job-economics floor sliced out of the $55 account minimum-worthwhile-charge program total — it must NEVER be used as a comparable, anchor, or benchmark for any future Elliott item, including future tiny labels, mid-size labels, or one-off requests. Future one-offs price from their own job economics (material + production time), not from this number.

**Methodology:** Job economics for a one-off small-batch run. Built from material cost + production time, anchored to the account's one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored).

**Job inputs:**

| Component | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Material cost (full 6-label job) | ~$1.33 (0.5 sq ft Orajet 3951 + 0.5 sq ft laminate at $0.2389/sq ft + ~$0.60 ink for 3-color ANSI Z535 — laminate updated 2026-05-28) |
| File prep / artwork prep / design time / pre-press | **$0** — Elliott supplies production-ready DWG (account-level rule, see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) |
| Production time (file import + print/lam/cut/inspect/package) | ~20-25 min total (5 min file import + 15-20 min run) |
| Lamination passes | 1 |
| Cut runs | 1 |

**Why the sq ft band is inapplicable.** At 0.026 sq ft, this label is well outside the ~0.5–2.0 sq ft singles band scope. Applying the singles band ($15.43/sq ft at qty 20) would yield $0.40/label — does not cover the run setup and inspection time. The band applies to mid-size production singles, not one-off small-batch jobs of this size.

**Why pricing anchors to the $55 account floor.** $55 is the lowest first article price on the account (P/N 1230820 FA) and serves as a program-level one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) — the lowest number Pro Label will run a one-off custom job for, regardless of how small. Anchoring at this floor:

- Program revenue: $55
- Material cost: ~$1.70
- Production time: ~20-25 min
- Contribution margin: ~$53 to cover ~20-25 minutes of machine + operator time + overhead
- That is the worthwhile-charge threshold for a 6-label one-off at this scale; below it the job is not worth running

**Why this is a one-off, not a catalog item.** Qty 6 for a single unit in the field. No recurring production runway. The tier table is flat at $9.17 for frontmatter completeness only — there is no volume curve when there is no expected volume.

**Why this is NOT a benchmark:**

- One-off, not catalog. No reorder runway.
- The per-sq-ft implied rate (~$352/sq ft) is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing a one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) by a small label area — structurally meaningless as a per-sq-ft reference.
- The per-label rate ($9.17) is the arithmetic byproduct of dividing $55 by 6 — it scales inversely with order quantity and is not a property of the label itself.
- Using this item to anchor a future quote would either over-price (if the future job has more labels to amortize $55 across) or under-price (if the future job has fewer labels or different setup).
- Future one-off small-batch jobs must rebuild job economics from scratch on their actual footprint and order quantity, then anchor to whichever account floor is appropriate at the time. Do NOT shortcut through this item.

**Tier structure:** All six tiers flattened to $9.17. There is no volume tier for a one-off — qty 6, no reorder. The flat tier table is a structural placeholder; the operational price is the program total of $55.

**Profile Validation:** Outside the Printed + Laminated singles Pricing Profile band (~0.5–2.0 sq ft scope) by design. The **price itself is NOT a Profile data point** and must NOT be fed into the singles band, the kit band, or any other band.

**AI Validation:** None. Per session brief — straightforward one-off job-economics call with no precedent-setting risk to existing bands. The four-round, six-model validation methodology is reserved for items that set precedent or could affect the existing pricing structure; this item is explicitly excluded from being a benchmark.

**Override:** None.

---
sections.margin_analysis:
> **N/A in the conventional recurring-production sense — this is a one-off priced at a flat $55 total, not a catalog item.** See Pricing Derivation for full job-cost reasoning. The margin table below is structural; the operationally meaningful metric is contribution per production-minute.

| Qty Tier | Price | Material Cost | Gross Profit | Margin |
|----------|-------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| 1-9 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |
| 10-19 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |
| 20-49 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |
| 50-99 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |
| 100-199 | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |
| 200+ | $9.17 | $0.22 | $8.95 | ~98% |

| Job-Level Metric | Value |
|------------------|-------|
| Program revenue (6 labels) | $55.00 |
| Material cost (full job) | ~$1.33 |
| Production time | ~20-25 min |
| File prep / artwork prep | $0 (Elliott provides production-ready DWG) |
| Gross margin (revenue − material) | ~$53.67 ≈ 98% of revenue |
| Contribution per minute of production time | ~$53.67 ÷ ~22 min ≈ $2.44/min ≈ $146/hr |

**Interpretation:** Margin % is structurally inflated because the price floor is set by the worthwhile-charge floor, not by a catalog rate. Contribution per production-minute (~$145/hr) is healthy for a one-off small-batch custom job. The internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%) is a recurring-production margin and is not directly comparable to a one-off — this $55 program has no repeat-run amortization to lean on, and its economics are best evaluated as contribution per machine-minute rather than as a percentage.

> ## ⚠ ONE-OFF ORDER
>
> Qty 6 for a unit in the field. No reorder expected or implied. This item exists as a single 6-label minimum-run order. The $55 program total is the only operationally relevant number.

> ## ⚠ DO NOT BENCHMARK
>
> $9.17/label is an arithmetic artifact of dividing the $55 account one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) by 6 labels. It is **never** to be used as anchor, comparable, or catalog rate for any future Elliott item — tiny label, mid-size label, or otherwise. The implied ~$352/sq ft is meaningless as a per-sq-ft reference. The per-label rate scales inversely with order quantity; it is not a property of the label. If any future session reaches for this item as a benchmark, STOP and re-read this section.

> ## ⚠ DO NOT SURFACE PER-LABEL MATH
>
> Quote as a single program line: "**one-time minimum program charge: $55.00 total**." The $9.17/label per-label number should not appear in customer-facing communication for this order. If asked to break it out, reframe as a program charge. Surfacing the per-label math invites a per-sq-ft comparison that this one-off cannot defend.

> ## ⚠ REORDER PRICING
>
> If this P/N is ever reordered, **reprice from scratch at production quantities.** Do not extrapolate the $9.17/label one-off rate. A recurring production order at qty 20+ would be validated against the singles sq ft band — at 0.026 sq ft × $15.43/sq ft, the band yields ~$0.40/label, which is below the worthwhile-charge threshold for tiny labels. The reorder price would be set by the job-economics floor for the actual order quantity, not by linear extrapolation from this one-off.

>

This is account spec-drift terminology — the actual material is the standard 1-mil polyester (Mylar) overlaminate used across the Orajet 3951 material family. **Interpretation:** Margin % is structurally inflated because the price floor is set by the worthwhile-charge floor, not by a catalog rate, and because material is near-zero at this size. Contribution per production-minute (~$149/hr) is healthy for a one-off small-batch custom job. The internal P&L benchmark (~79.7%) is a recurring-production margin and is not directly comparable to a one-off — this $55 program has no repeat-run amortization to lean on, and its economics are best evaluated as contribution per machine-minute rather than as a percentage.
