sections.notes:
> ## ⚠ ONE-OFF ORDER
>
> Qty 6 for a field service request. No reorder expected or implied. Arrived alongside P/N 3017583 as part of the same field service request (next-day rush) — they are separate line items, not a kit. This item exists as a single 6-label minimum-run order. The $55 program total is the only operationally relevant number.

> ## ⚠ DO NOT BENCHMARK
>
> $9.17/label is an arithmetic artifact of dividing the $55 account one-off job-economics floor (1230820 FA-anchored) by 6 labels. It is **never** to be used as anchor, comparable, or catalog rate for any future Elliott item — tiny label, mid-size label, or otherwise. The implied ~$5,270/sq ft is the most extreme per-sq-ft artifact on the account and is meaningless as a reference. The per-label rate scales inversely with order quantity; it is not a property of the label. If any future session reaches for this item as a benchmark, STOP and re-read this section.

> ## ⚠ SMALLEST ITEM ON THE ACCOUNT
>
> At 0.5" × 0.5" = 0.00174 sq ft, this is the smallest item on the entire Elliott account. The sq ft singles band is structurally inapplicable at this scale — any sq-ft-based price would be cents per label and far below the worthwhile-charge floor. Any future Elliott item at or near this size must be priced from job economics, not from a band.

> ## ⚠ DO NOT SURFACE PER-LABEL MATH
>
> Quote as a single program line: "**one-time minimum program charge: $55.00 total**." The $9.17/label per-label number should not appear in customer-facing communication for this order. If asked to break it out, reframe as a program charge. Surfacing the per-label math invites a per-sq-ft comparison that this one-off cannot defend.

> ## ⚠ REORDER PRICING
>
> If this P/N is ever reordered, **reprice from scratch at production quantities.** Do not extrapolate the $9.17/label one-off rate. A recurring production order at qty 20+ would be validated against the singles sq ft band — at 0.00174 sq ft × $15.43/sq ft, the band yields ~$0.03/label, which is far below the worthwhile-charge threshold for tiny labels. The reorder price would be set by the job-economics floor for the actual order quantity, not by linear extrapolation from this one-off.

> ## ⚠ ACCOUNT FLOOR
>
> $55 is the validated minimum-worthwhile-charge for one-off jobs on this account (anchored to P/N 1230820 FA price). Do not go below this on future similar jobs. Future one-off small-batch programs anchor at this floor or higher, never below.

> ## ⚠ MYLAR TERMINOLOGY
>
> Drawing callout reads "1 mil clear Mylar overlay." Mylar is a polyester-film trade name (originally DuPont) — this maps directly to the standard 1-mil polyester overlaminate used across this account's Orajet 3951 material family. No material change; brand-term-vs-generic alignment only. Sister item P/N 3017583 uses "1 mil clear vinyl overlay" wording on its drawing for the same physical laminate — Elliott is actively working with Pro Label to standardize spec-sheet verbiage per Nick's account context.

> ## ⚠ ARTWORK PREP
>
> Elliott supplies production-ready DWG files on **every** job. File prep, artwork prep, design time, and pre-press cost are **always zero** on this account — past, present, and future. See `.claude/MASTER_CONTEXT.md` Core Rule #7 and `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

sections.nesting:
## Roll Specs

| Spec | Value |
|------|-------|
| Roll | 3M Controltac 180mC-57 Olympic Blue, 24" × 10yd |
| Roll cost | $162.78 |
| Roll sq in | 24" × 360" = 8,640 sq in |
| Cost per sq in | $162.78 ÷ 8,640 = $0.018839/sq in |
| Cost per sq ft | $162.78 ÷ 60 sq ft = $2.713/sq ft |
| Cost per yard | $162.78 ÷ 10 yd = $16.278/yd |

## Nesting Layout on 24" Roll

| Dimension | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Label width (across roll) | 11.00" |
| Labels across 24" roll | 2 (2 × 11" = 22", leaves 2" waste strip) |
| Label length (down roll) | 32.88" = 0.9133 yd |
| Positions along 360" roll | 10 (360 ÷ 32.88 = 10.95 → 10 complete) |
| **Labels per roll** | **20 (2 across × 10 positions)** |
| Unused at roll end | 360" − (10 × 32.88") = 31.2" |
| Roll-end waste | 8.7% of roll length |
| Side waste | 2" strip (9.1% of roll width) |

> **Yield Note:** The session brief indicated "10 labels per roll (2 across × 5 positions)." Verified math: 360" ÷ 32.88" = 10.95 → 10 full positions. Correct yield is **20 labels per roll**. Material cost per label (calculated from $/sq in × allocated area) is unaffected by this discrepancy.

## Material Cost per Label

**Vinyl (length-based, includes blade path allocation):**

| Step | Value |
|------|-------|
| Roll width allocated per label | 24" ÷ 2 = 12" (2-across layout) |
| Label length | 32.88" = 0.9133 yd |
| Cost per row of 2 labels | 0.9133 yd × $16.278/yd = $14.864 |
| Vinyl cost per label | $14.864 ÷ 2 = **$7.43** |

**Application Tape (length-based):**

| Step | Value |
|------|-------|
| Feed length per row | 32.88" + 6" spacing = 38.88" = 1.0800 yd |
| Tape cost per row of 2 labels | 1.0800 yd × $1.1821/yd = $1.277 |
| Tape cost per label | $1.277 ÷ 2 = **$0.638** |

**Total Material Cost per Label: $7.433 + $0.638 = $8.071 → $8.07**

| Component | Cost |
|-----------|------|
| Vinyl (3M 180mC-57 Olympic Blue) | $7.433 |
| Application tape (TransferRite 582U) | $0.638 |
| **Total material cost per label** | **$8.071 → $8.07** |

> **Tape cost corrected 2026-06-09** from area method (pseudo-rate: 2.512 sq ft × $0.5911/sq ft = $1.485) to the length-based method for consistency with Band B, Band C, and 3010701. No sell price change.

> **No 48" roll option available for this color.** Olympic Blue is only stocked at 24" × 10yd. If higher volume warrants it, a 48" roll quote from 3M would reduce material cost — document as an operational improvement opportunity, not current pricing basis.

sections.production:
## Process Steps

1. **File import/setup** — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to cutter. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. **Cut** — Roland SG3-300 on 24" Olympic Blue stock. 2 labels across, cut in rows. Estimated: 5-8 min per production run.
3. **Weed** — remove excess vinyl around "D115" block letters. 4 large characters, no fine detail, open geometry. Estimated: 10-15 min per unit at small quantities; 5-8 min/unit at production volume.
4. **Mask** — apply TransferRite 582U transfer tape over weeded characters. Squeegee for adhesion. Estimated: 3-5 min per label.
5. **Inspect** — verify cut accuracy, confirm no lifted edges, check character legibility.
6. **Package** — flat, masked with transfer tape carrier. Label is delivered ready for hand-application on equipment.

## Production Notes

- **Lamination passes:** 0 (cut vinyl, no print)
- **Cut runs:** 1
- **Equipment:** Roland SG3-300 (24" stock)
- **Weeding complexity:** Low. "D115" is 4 large block characters at generous scale. No serifs, no fine interior detail. Similar weeding profile to "E190" (P/N 1205720).
- **Weeding time:** Faster than the 7-character "ELLIOTT" brand lettering (P/N 3017435). Comparable to P/N 1205720.

## Estimated Production Time per Unit

| Step | Time (small qty) | Time (production vol) |
|------|-----------------|----------------------|
| File import/setup | ~5 min mechanical, NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22) | — |
| Cut | 8 min | 5 min |
| Weed | 15 min | 8 min |
| Mask (transfer tape) | 5 min | 3 min |
| Inspect + pack | 5 min | 3 min |
| **Per-unit billable total** | **~33 min** | **~19 min** |

**Override:** None. Engine output.

Rule 14 deviation acknowledged: P/N 1205720 is a Relationship Concession. This item prices within the concession-phase band by deliberate strategic choice, not oversight. If/when relationship pricing normalizes, recalibrate against the AI consensus range ($14.84–$16.41/sq ft).

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-06-30 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves). Final price locked by Nick.**
>
> **This is NOT a direct parity exemption.** P/N 3020477 is an independently validated sub-scope band data point (`do_not_benchmark: false`) that happens to land on a tier ladder identical to P/N 1247120's by deliberate pricing decision — not because its dimensions, material, or process were parity-cloned. Dimensions (0.130 sq ft vs 1247120's 0.122 sq ft) and material cost ($0.25 vs $0.25, coincidentally equal) were independently derived from this item's own spec sheet. See `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md` for the distinction between a direct parity exemption (no AI validation run, validation inherited) and this item, which ran the full process and arrived at the same ladder through evidence.

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 3020477 LBL-MODULAR BOOM CONTROL HYDRAULIC CONTROL (in-label artwork: "CONTROL SELECTOR"). 11.50" × 1.63" = 0.1302 sq ft (filed 0.130). Single label, 1 piece. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. Non-ANSI control/selector label. 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope routing (Decision Tree Step 1a from `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`):**

| Threshold | Value | Result |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| sq ft < 0.1? | 0.130 ≥ 0.1 | NOT Micro-Format Band |
| 0.1 ≤ sq ft < 0.5? | 0.130 in range | **SUB-SCOPE HANDLING** |
| sq ft ≥ 0.5? | 0.130 < 0.5 | NOT Singles Band |

**Routing: Sub-scope (0.1–0.5 sq ft).** Price at band-consistent $/sq ft with small-format premium per the sub-scope doctrine established by P/N 1210810. This is the **fourth** confirmed sub-scope data point, joining 1247120 (0.122 sq ft), 1279130 (0.148 sq ft), and 1210810 (0.292 sq ft).

---

## Step 2 — Governing Comparables and Benchmark Comparison

Bracketing comparables: **P/N 1247120** (0.122 sq ft, nearest sub-scope item below) and **P/N 1279130** (0.148 sq ft, nearest sub-scope item above).

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft (qty 20) | Notes |
|------|-------|----------------|-----------------|-------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root |
| 1210810 (upper sub-scope bracket) | 0.292 | $4.75 | $16.27 | Sub-scope — INTENTIONALLY ABOVE band |
| 1279130 (upper bracket) | 0.148 | $3.10 | $20.95 | Sub-scope |
| **3020477 (this item)** | **0.130** | **$2.75** | **$21.15** | **Sub-scope — between brackets, monotonic** |
| 1247120 (lower bracket) | 0.122 | $2.75 | $22.54 | Sub-scope |
| 1279000 (Micro founding) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | Micro-Format Band |

**Key structural observations:**
1. **Sub-scope gradient confirmed monotonic across four points:** $22.54 (0.122 sq ft) → $21.15 (0.130 sq ft) → $20.95 (0.148 sq ft) → $16.27 (0.292 sq ft). As area increases, $/sq ft decreases — structurally correct.
2. **Fourth confirmed sub-scope data point:** This item fills the narrow gap between 1247120 and 1279130, tightening the sub-scope gradient.
3. **Absolute-price equality with 1247120 is a validated outcome, not an assumption.** Both price at $2.75 at qty 20 despite different sq ft (0.130 vs 0.122) — confirmed correct because the $/sq ft at this position ($21.15) sits properly between the two brackets; the equal dollar price is the result of independent validation landing on the same $0.25 snap point as 1247120, not the cause of it.
4. **Quote email anchor line:** "Pricing is consistent with the 1247120 comp you've already approved."

---

## Step 3 — Material Cost and $/sq ft Validation

**Material cost per §25 canonical formula:**

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.130 × $1.21 | $0.1573 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.130 × $0.2389 | $0.0311 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 0.130 × $0.50 | $0.0650 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.2534** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) | judgment-applied | − $0.0034 |
| **Canonical material cost** | | **$0.25** |

**$/sq ft at qty 20:** $2.75 / 0.130 = **$21.15/sq ft**

This is:
- **$1.39/sq ft below 1247120** ($22.54 − $21.15 = −6.2%) — correctly lower on a larger sub-scope label
- **$0.20/sq ft above 1279130** ($21.15 − $20.95 = +1.0%) — correctly higher on a smaller sub-scope label
- **$5.24–$5.72/sq ft above the singles band** ($21.15 vs $15.43–$15.91) — sub-scope premium above the band, not below

---

## Step 4 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave (Wave 2 had 1 non-response — 5/6). Atomic session model: each prompt self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Nick is sole decision-maker; the responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build Round — independent anchors, no cross-model exposure):** 6/6 models built pricing independently from this item's own specs, material cost, and the sub-scope comparable set (1247120, 1279130, 1210810). **4/6 landed at $2.75 at qty 20**, anchoring directly to 1247120's ladder as the nearest sub-scope neighbor by area. **2/6 landed at $3.00**, reasoning from a steeper gradient-interpolated-upward read of the curve between 1247120 and 1279000 (Micro-Format Band).

**Wave 2 (Destruction Round — 5/6 responded, one model stalled/non-responsive):** **Unanimous kill of $3.00** among responding models. The Buyer/Procurement attack and the Strategic attack — independently, by different models — both converged on the same structural flaw: $3.00 at qty 20 = $23.08/sq ft (0.130 sq ft), which **exceeds 1247120's $22.54/sq ft despite 3020477 being the larger label** — a direct gradient inversion (a larger sub-scope label must carry an equal-or-lower $/sq ft than a smaller one, not higher). $3.00 was eliminated outright by both attack vectors at High severity. The two surviving candidates split among the 5 responding models: **3/5 recommended $2.75** (exact dollar match to 1247120, zero gradient risk), **2/5 recommended $2.85** (the strict $/sq ft-interpolated value between 1247120 and 1279130, ≈ $21.92/sq ft).

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation as Sean Finn):** **6/6 unanimous.** All six Sean-Finn simulations rubber-stamp $2.75 same-day, zero friction, citing the direct dollar match to 1247120 as an instantly recognizable, already-approved number — the quote email anchor line referencing 1247120 directly closes the loop before Sean does the math himself. By contrast, every simulation that was shown $2.85 generated a polite clarification question ("why is this five cents different from the others on this drawing set?") — **not PO-blocking, but friction that $2.75 does not introduce.**

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis, cold atomic sessions with full dossier):** **6/6 unanimous YES on $2.75, full ladder unmodified.** The decisive argument, independently converged on by multiple models: $2.85's mathematical precision (closer fit to the strict interpolated $/sq ft curve) is outweighed by two factors — **(a)** it introduces buyer friction (Wave 3 clarification question) for no material benefit (the dollar delta is $0.10/label, immaterial at any realistic order volume), and **(b)** it visibly exposes Pro Label's interpolation methodology to a buyer who is actively co-developing a procurement/engineering labeling standard with Pro Label — handing Sean a reverse-engineerable pricing framework at the worst possible moment in the relationship. $2.75 — the exact 1247120 match — closes off that exposure entirely while costing nothing in margin terms that matters.

**Nick's decision:** Locked the full tier table at $2.75 at qty 20, identical to 1247120's ladder, per Wave 4 unanimous consensus. No override type — engine consensus accepted.

---

| 1279000 (Micro founding) | 0.097 | $3.00 | Micro-Format Band ($30.86/sq ft) |

**$/sq ft monotonicity across sub-scope and micro items (by area, descending):**
- 1210810 (0.292 sq ft): $16.27/sq ft
- 1279130 (0.148 sq ft): $20.95/sq ft
- **3020477 (0.130 sq ft): $21.15/sq ft** ← this item, correctly between 1279130 and 1247120

- 1279000 (0.097 sq ft): $30.86/sq ft

The $/sq ft gradient is monotonically increasing as area decreases across all four sub-scope points plus the Micro-Format Band founding point — structurally correct.

The validation process arrived at $2.75 at qty 20 through evidence (Wave 1 build, Wave 2 destruction, Wave 3 buyer simulation, Wave 4 synthesis) and landed on a tier ladder identical to 1247120's as a **deliberate strategic decision** documented in Pricing Derivation Step 4 — Wave 4's decisive finding was that the mathematically "more precise" $2.85 alternative introduced buyer friction and exposed Pro Label's interpolation methodology to Sean for a $0.10/label difference that does not matter at any realistic order volume.
>
> Future sessions should not describe this item as a "parity clone" — that classification belongs to items where multi-round AI validation was waived entirely per `governance/PRICING_VALIDATION.md` (e.g., 1068270, 1278220, 1277300, 1279020). This item ran the full process.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 2.000" width and 1.375" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Either orientation yields a single lamination pass.

- 2.000" across laminator: 11.500" clearance — single pass ✓
- 1.375" across laminator: 12.125" clearance — single pass ✓

### Interior Cutout

The Ø1/2" circle cutout is handled as a standard Roland contour cut path, defined in the Elliott-supplied DWG registration marks. The loose 1/2" disc is weeded as waste after cutting. No separate punch, die, or post-process operation required. This is a standard cut path for the Roland SG3-300.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 2.000" × 1.375". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

**Orientation A** (2.000" across the 28" bed, 1.375" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 14 (14 × 2.000" = 28.000" = exact fit — confirm clearance on live run)
- Conservative: 13 per row (13 × 2.000" = 26.000" ≤ 28", 2" clearance) ✓

**Orientation B** (1.375" across the 28" bed, 2.000" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 20 (20 × 1.375" = 27.500" ≤ 28") ✓

Nesting is extremely efficient at any expected order quantity. Many labels per row; typical order qty 20 fits in 1–2 rows.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-06-16. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22; 1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28; full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01).
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.019 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.023 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.019 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.005 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.019 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.010 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.038** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.062 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.10** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste on every Elliott printed/laminated item. The buffer is judgment-applied, not a fixed number. For this item the calculated $0.038 rounds to $0.10 — consistent with P/N 3024592 (calculated $0.1052, filed $0.20) in that material is negligible at this size class and the incidental buffer dominates over the raw calculation. At 0.019 sq ft — the smallest item on the account — $0.10 is the minimum defensible material cost floor.

sections.production:
1. File import/setup — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG (registration marks and Ø1/2" circle contour cut path already defined), send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22).
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, black eco-solvent on Orajet 3951 white. Black text on white background, full bleed standard.
3. Laminate — 1-mil clear polyester. ONE pass (both 2.000" and 1.375" dimensions fit the 13.5" laminator with ample clearance in either orientation).
4. Contour cut — Roland follows registration marks. Cut path includes: (a) outer perimeter with R1/4 radius corners; (b) interior Ø1/2" circle cutout.
5. Weed — remove outer excess material AND the interior 1/2" disc (disc is the waste from the circle cutout). Both weeding operations are simple and fast. Interior disc pops out cleanly on the Roland contour cut.
6. Inspect — print quality (black on white, single-color), laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, corner radius, Ø1/2" circle cleanliness.
7. Package — flat, stacked.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Estimated Production Time (batch of 20):** ~15–20 min total (5 min file import + 10–15 min print/lam/cut/weed/inspect/package). Highly efficient — tiny label, simple single-color content, no ANSI compliance inspection required.
- **Constraints:** None. All dimensions well within equipment limits. Interior circle weeding adds ~5–10 seconds per label vs a perimeter-only cut. No die, no punch, no special fixturing.
- **Note on complexity vs 3024592:** This item does NOT require ANSI color registration, regulated format compliance checking, or compliance inspection (no red/yellow ANSI panels, no pictogram fidelity check, no regulated text format). That eliminated overhead exceeds the minor additional weeding time for the Ø1/2" interior disc.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-06-16 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves). Final price locked by Nick.**
>
> This item is the **smallest item on the Elliott account at 0.019 sq ft**. The per-label floor governs AND is **complexity-dependent**. The key insight confirmed by 4-wave validation: this simple non-ANSI control label floors **below** P/N 3024592 ($2.75, ANSI Z535.6 DANGER) at **$2.50 at qty 20**, despite being smaller in area.

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 3024140 LBL-WRK LGHTS. 2.000" × 1.375" = 0.01910 sq ft (filed 0.019). Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. Non-ANSI, simple black-on-white control label. Interior Ø1/2" circle cutout (standard contour cut). 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope:** 0.019 sq ft is below 0.1 sq ft — routes to the Micro-Format Band for production catalog items. This item is a **production catalog item** (not a one-off / field-service request), so the Micro-Format Band framework governs, not the $55 tiny one-off floor.

**Sub-band position:** 0.019 sq ft is below the ~0.06 sq ft floor caution threshold documented in 1279000 and confirmed by 3024592. At this size, the linear $/sq ft formula produces an unworkable per-label number:

| Method | Result at qty 20 |
|--------|-----------------|
| Linear Micro-Format Band ($30.86/sq ft × 0.019 sq ft) | $0.59 |
| Per-label floor — complexity-dependent | **$2.50 (non-ANSI) / $2.75 (ANSI)** |
| Governs | **Per-label floor (complexity-dependent)** |

The linear formula at $0.59 is inapplicable — below any viable per-label floor by a factor of ~4×. The per-label floor governs. The floor is further determined to be **complexity-dependent** (new insight confirmed by this item's 4-wave validation — see Step 3 below).

---

## Step 2 — Benchmark Comparison

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft | Complexity Class | Method |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|-----------------|--------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | N/A | Singles band root |
| 1279000 (Micro-Format Band founding) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | ANSI Z535.4 WARNING | Micro-Format Band anchor |
| 1278220 (Parity clone of 1279000) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | ANSI Z535.4 WARNING | Inherited from 1279000 |
| 3024592 (sub-0.06 sq ft floor anchor) | 0.054 | $2.75 | $50.93 | ANSI Z535.6 DANGER | Per-label floor (ANSI) |
| **3024140 (this item)** | **0.019** | **$2.50** | **$131.58** | **Non-ANSI — simple control label** | **Per-label floor (non-ANSI) — $/sq ft is artifact only** |

Note: the $131.58/sq ft figure for 3024140 is an arithmetic byproduct of the per-label floor divided by the label area. It is NOT the pricing basis and must NEVER be used as a $/sq ft reference for future items. The governing number is **$2.50 per label at qty 20**. Future non-ANSI micro control labels should reference this item; future ANSI regulated micro labels should reference 3024592 ($2.75) or 1279000 ($3.00).

---

## Step 3 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 total independent responses. Atomic session model: each prompt self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Nick is sole decision-maker; the 24 responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build Round):** All 6 models built pricing from specs, material cost, and benchmark data (primary comparable: 3024592 at $2.75, 0.054 sq ft, ANSI Z535.6 DANGER). All 6 confirmed that the linear formula ($0.59) is inapplicable. All 6 initially proposed **$2.75 at qty 20** — mirroring 3024592, invoking the per-label floor, and citing the complexity offset: the interior Ø1/2" circle cutout adds production labor vs 3024592's simpler perimeter-only cut, offsetting 3024592's ANSI color complexity. Wave 1 consensus: $2.75, tier table mirroring 3024592.

**Wave 2 (Destruction Round):** All 6 models rated High on all four attack vectors. The primary structural finding: **the Wave 1 complexity offset argument does not hold under scrutiny.** 
- The Ø1/2" circle cutout adds approximately 5–10 seconds of contour weeding per label (the disc pops out cleanly; this is a standard Roland cut path with minimal weeding complexity).
- Eliminating ANSI Z535.6 compliance on 3024592 removes: multi-color registration (red header panel must meet ANSI Z535.6 color spec), regulated format compliance checking (mandatory ANSI layout, signal word positioning, pictogram fidelity), and compliance inspection per label (color fidelity, contrast, signal word accuracy). This eliminated overhead materially exceeds the ~5–10 seconds of interior disc weeding.
- 6/6 rejected the complexity offset argument: the ANSI compliance labor savings dominate. The true per-label floor for a simple black-on-white non-ANSI control label is BELOW the floor for an ANSI regulated label at the same size class.
- Buyer attack vector: if Sean ever compares these two line items, the price relationship is logically defensible — a simpler label costs less than a regulated safety label, regardless of the area ordering. The complexity justification is transparent and sound.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation):** Models simulated Sean Finn as an experienced industrial procurement buyer with ~$140K annual label spend. Context: 3024592 ($2.75) already in Sean's file; this item presented for comparison.
- 3/6 simulations approved at $2.75 (immediate PO, no questions — these models emphasized that Sean may not examine the labels at this price level, rubber stamps at $2.75).
- 3/6 questioned or pushed back: pushback threshold identified at $2.25–$2.50; instant approval range $1.50–$2.25. These models emphasized that Sean will eventually compare 3024140 (non-ANSI, simpler label) against 3024592 (ANSI DANGER, more complex) — if both are $2.75, he may question why the simpler label isn't cheaper.
- Net assessment: $2.75 is defensible but exposes a buyer-side logic gap that materializes over time as Sean builds a mental model of the account's pricing structure.

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis):** Cold atomic sessions, full dossier including Wave 2 complexity offset rejection.
- **6/6 unanimous NO on $2.75 at qty 20** — Wave 2 complexity offset destruction accepted; $2.75 mirroring 3024592 perpetuates an internally inconsistent price relationship (simpler label, same price as regulated ANSI label, smaller area — all three factors argue for a lower price).
- **6/6 unanimous YES on $2.50 at qty 20** — $2.50 is the correct floor for a simple non-ANSI control label at this size class. It sits $0.25 below 3024592, creating a visible and defensible step-down for complexity differentiation. The tier table shifted down uniformly vs 3024592 at every tier.
- Quote framing confirmed: *"Same material and process as P/N 3024592 — simpler label content, adjusted price."*

**Nick's decision:** Locked $2.50 at qty 20. Engine consensus accepted; no override. Tier table: $4.00 / $3.00 / $2.50 / $2.25 / $2.00 / $1.75.

---

## Step 4 — Complexity-Dependent Per-Label Floor Documentation

> **CRITICAL RULE FOR FUTURE SESSIONS:** The per-label floor at the sub-0.06 sq ft tier is now confirmed to be **COMPLEXITY-DEPENDENT**, not a single fixed number.
>
> | Label Complexity Class | Per-Label Floor (qty 20) | Reference Item |
> |------------------------|--------------------------|----------------|
> | ANSI regulated (multi-color, compliance inspection) | **$2.75** | 3024592 (ANSI Z535.6 DANGER) |
> | Simple non-ANSI control label (single/dual color, no compliance inspection) | **$2.50** | 3024140 (this item) |
>
> Future sub-0.06 sq ft production items must:
> 1. Calculate the linear Micro-Format Band price ($30.86/sq ft × area)
> 2. If linear < complexity-appropriate floor → use the floor
> 3. Determine complexity class (ANSI regulated vs simple non-ANSI)
> 4. Apply the appropriate floor ($2.75 ANSI or $2.50 non-ANSI)
> 5. Run 4-wave AI validation to confirm the specific floor price — do NOT clone mechanically
>
> Do NOT use 3024140's $2.50 or 3024592's $2.75 as mechanical parity clones for future items — validate fresh.

---

sections.notes:
> ## SMALLEST ITEM ON THE ACCOUNT AT 0.019 SQ FT
>
> P/N 3024140 at 0.019 sq ft is the **smallest production catalog printed/laminated item** on the Elliott account. The implied $/sq ft rate ($131.58 at qty 20) is a **mathematical artifact only** — NOT a pricing rate. Never use this figure as a $/sq ft benchmark for any future item, for any purpose.
>
> The governing metric is **$2.50 per label at qty 20** — period.

---

> ## THIS ITEM PRICES BELOW 3024592 ($2.75) DESPITE BEING SMALLER — COMPLEXITY DIFFERENTIATION
>
> P/N 3024592 (ANSI Z535.6 DANGER, 0.054 sq ft, $2.75 at qty 20) is a **larger** label that prices **higher** than this item. This is intentional and correct.
>
> **Why:** P/N 3024592 is a full ANSI Z535.6 DANGER regulated label requiring:
> - Multi-color print registration (red DANGER header to ANSI Z535.6 color spec)
> - Regulated format compliance (mandatory ANSI layout, signal word positioning, pictogram placement)
> - Compliance inspection per label (color fidelity, contrast, signal word accuracy, pictogram fidelity)
>
> P/N 3024140 is a simple black-on-white non-ANSI control label (+ WORK LIGHTS / ON / OFF) requiring:
> - Single-color black print on white background — no color registration
> - No regulated format — standard equipment control label layout
> - Standard quality inspection only — no ANSI compliance inspection
> - Interior Ø1/2" circle cutout adds ~5–10 seconds of contour weeding (disc pops out cleanly)
>
> **The eliminated ANSI compliance labor on 3024140 exceeds the interior circle weeding labor.** The complexity offset argument (advanced in Wave 1 by all 6 models) was unanimously rejected by Wave 2 and Wave 4.
>
> **Future pricing implications:**
> - Future **simple non-ANSI micro control labels** should reference 3024140 ($2.50 at qty 20).
> - Future **ANSI regulated micro labels** should reference 3024592 ($2.75 at qty 20) or 1279000 ($3.00 at qty 20, ANSI Z535.4 WARNING).

---

> ## COMPLEXITY-DEPENDENT PER-LABEL FLOOR — CONFIRMED 2026-06-16
>
> The per-label floor below ~0.06 sq ft is now confirmed to be **complexity-dependent**, not a single fixed number. Two live production data points:
>
> | Label Type | Item | Sq Ft | Floor (qty 20) |
> |------------|------|-------|---------------|
> | ANSI regulated (DANGER class) | 3024592 | 0.054 | $2.75 |
> | Non-ANSI control label | 3024140 | 0.019 | $2.50 |
>
> The $0.25 step-down between them reflects the ANSI compliance overhead differential. Future sessions must classify a new sub-0.06 sq ft item's complexity before applying the floor.

---

> ## INTERIOR Ø1/2" CIRCLE CUTOUT — STANDARD ROLAND CONTOUR CUT
>
> Drawing shows an interior Ø1/2" (0.500") circle cutout. This is a **standard Roland SG3-300 contour cut path** — the circle is defined in the Elliott-supplied DWG registration marks. No separate punch, die, tool change, or post-process operation required.
>
> The loose 1/2" disc is weeded as waste after the Roland cuts. Weeding time: ~5–10 seconds per label. This is the standard method for all interior cutout features on the Roland platform.

---

> ## SPLIT LINER — STANDARD, NO PROCESS IMPACT
>
> Drawing note 2 specifies "peel-off backing slit at middle" — a standard split liner (also called easy-peel or slit liner). The liner is slit down the center, allowing the end user to peel each half independently for easier application to curved or hard-to-reach surfaces.
>
> The split liner is supplied by the Orajet 3951 substrate — no additional slitting operation is required from Pro Label. No process impact, no cost impact, no quoting impact. Standard for this material.

---

> ## §26 INVOICE PROTECTION — TIER BOUNDARY VERIFICATION
>
> All five tier boundaries verified clean — see §26 table in Pricing Derivation. All cliffs resolved automatically at billing time by §26 invoice protection — no quote-email language required.

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. Step 1 (file import/setup) is a ~5-minute mechanical operation. This is a permanent account-level rule. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24.

---

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE (4-WAVE ATOMIC PROCESS)
>
> 4-wave, 6-model atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md` is complete (24 total independent responses). Full documentation in the Pricing Derivation section.
>
> **Key outcomes:**
> - Wave 1 (Build): 6/6 models proposed $2.75 mirroring 3024592, citing interior cutout complexity offset.
> - Wave 2 (Destruction): 6/6 H on all four attack vectors. Complexity offset rejected: ANSI compliance labor savings exceed interior circle weeding labor (~5–10 sec). True non-ANSI floor is below $2.75.
> - Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation): 3/6 approved at $2.75 (rubber stamp); 3/6 questioned or pushed back — pushback threshold $2.25–$2.50; instant approval $1.50–$2.25.
> - Wave 4 (Final Synthesis): 6/6 unanimous NO on $2.75; 6/6 unanimous YES on $2.50 at qty 20. Engine consensus accepted, no override.
> - Nick locked $2.50 at qty 20 with the full tier table as validated.

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

Both the 4.750" width and 1.625" height dimensions are well within the 13.5" polyester laminator. Either orientation yields a single lamination pass.

- 4.750" across laminator: 8.750" clearance — single pass ✓
- 1.625" across laminator: 11.875" clearance — single pass ✓

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 4.750" × 1.625". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28".

**Orientation A** (4.750" across the 28" bed, 1.625" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 5 (5 × 4.750" = 23.75" ≤ 28")
- At qty 20: 4 rows × 5 labels = 20 labels in one print run ✓

**Orientation B** (1.625" across the 28" bed, 4.750" in feed direction):
- Labels per row: 17 (17 × 1.625" = 27.625" ≤ 28")
- At qty 20: 2 rows easily covers qty 20 ✓

Nesting is extremely efficient at any expected order quantity. Multiple labels per row, single print run at any expected order quantity.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-06-16. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22; 1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28; full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01).
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.054 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.0653 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.054 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.0129 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.054 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.0270 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.1052** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.0948 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.20** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste on every Elliott printed/laminated item. The buffer is judgment-applied, not a fixed number. For this item the calculated $0.1052 rounds to $0.20 — consistent with P/N 1279000 (calculated $0.1894, filed $0.20) despite the smaller area, because material is negligible at this size class and the incidental buffer dominates over the raw calculation.

sections.pricing_derivation:
> **VALIDATED 2026-06-16 — 4-WAVE ATOMIC AI VALIDATION COMPLETE (24 independent responses, 6 models × 4 waves). Final price locked by Nick.**
>
> This item is the **first sub-0.06 sq ft production catalog item** on the Elliott account. The Micro-Format Band linear formula ($30.86/sq ft × 0.054 sq ft = $1.67 at qty 20) is OVERRIDDEN by the per-label floor documented in the Micro-Format Band founding note (see P/N 1279000 and `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`). Floor anchor confirmed at **$2.75 at qty 20**. This IS a band data point for the sub-0.06 sq ft floor.

---

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 3024592 LBL-FALL PRTCT ANCHRG 1 PERSON. 4.750" × 1.625" = 0.0536 sq ft (filed 0.054). Single label. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. ANSI Z535.6 DANGER class. 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope:** 0.054 sq ft is below 0.1 sq ft — routes to the Micro-Format Band for production catalog items. This item is a **production catalog item** (not a one-off / field-service request), so the Micro-Format Band governs, not the $55 tiny one-off floor.

**Sub-band position:** 0.054 sq ft is also below the ~0.06 sq ft floor caution documented in 1279000 and the Micro-Format Band Pricing Profile. At this size, the linear $/sq ft formula ($30.86 × 0.054 = $1.67) yields a per-label price BELOW the structural per-label floor (~$2.50–$3.00 at qty 20). The floor overrides. This is the first time the floor has been tested on a live production item at this account.

**Linear formula vs. floor:**

| Method | Result at qty 20 |
|--------|-----------------|
| Linear Micro-Format Band ($30.86/sq ft × 0.054 sq ft) | $1.67 |
| Per-label floor (confirmed by 4-wave validation) | **$2.75** |
| Governs | **Per-label floor** |

The floor governs because fixed labor per label (file import, print setup, lamination pass, contour cut, weeding, ANSI compliance inspection, packaging) has a minimum that does not scale linearly with label area. At 0.054 sq ft — half the area of the 1279000 founding data point — these fixed costs amortize over even less material, making the $/sq ft formula produce an unworkable per-label number.

---

## Step 2 — Benchmark Comparison

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft | Method |
|------|-------|----------------|---------|--------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root |
| 1279000 (Micro-Format Band founding) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | Micro-Format Band anchor |
| 1278220 (Parity clone of 1279000) | 0.097 | $3.00 | $30.86 | Inherited from 1279000 |
| **3024592 (this item)** | **0.054** | **$2.75** | **$50.93** | **Per-label floor — $/sq ft is artifact only** |

Note: the $50.93/sq ft figure for 3024592 is an arithmetic byproduct of the per-label floor divided by the label area. It is NOT the pricing basis and should NOT be used as a $/sq ft reference for future items. The governing number is **$2.75 per label at qty 20**.

---

## Step 3 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 total independent responses. Atomic session model: each prompt self-contained; no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Nick is sole decision-maker; the 24 responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build Round):** Models independently built pricing from specs + material cost + benchmark data. All 6 confirmed that the linear $30.86/sq ft formula produces a price ($1.67) that falls below a defensible per-label floor at this area. All 6 anchored to the per-label floor logic documented in 1279000. Central tendency: $2.50–$3.00 at qty 20, with $2.75 as the modal recommendation — exactly at the midpoint of the floor range.

**Wave 2 (Destruction Round):** Primary finding: at 0.054 sq ft, any price below ~$2.50 at qty 20 fails buyer credibility (Sean compares against 1279000 at $3.00 for a label that is only ~1.8× the area). $/sq ft math does not help — the buyer will not do $/sq ft arithmetic on a $2.75 label. The per-label floor is the only defensible framing. The 200+ tier at $2.00 was noted as sitting below the floor band minimum but was accepted as structural completeness — same pattern as 1279000's 200+ at $2.10.

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation):** All 6 models simulating Sean Finn approved $2.75 at qty 20 without pushback. The label is compared against 1279000/1278220 ($3.00) — slightly smaller label, slightly lower price; the relationship is logical. No model simulated a pushback at $2.75. Quote email anchor line confirmed: *"Same material and process as P/N 1279000/1278220 — no file prep charge, no first article."*

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis):** All responses confirmed: send as shown. The per-label floor at $2.75 is the correct governing mechanism. The linear formula at $1.67 is structurally unsendable. The tier compression pattern is consistent with the Micro-Format Band (flatter than the singles band, appropriate for labor-dominant tiny labels). No modifications adopted.

**Nick's decision:** Locked the full tier table as validated. No override. Engine consensus accepted.

---

## Step 4 — Per-Label Floor Documentation

> **CRITICAL RULE FOR FUTURE SESSIONS:** The per-label floor (~$2.50–$3.00 at qty 20) is now CONFIRMED with a live production item at 0.054 sq ft. This item establishes the floor data point.
>
> Future sub-0.06 sq ft production items on this account must:
> 1. Calculate the linear Micro-Format Band price ($30.86/sq ft × area)
> 2. Compare to the per-label floor (~$2.50–$3.00 at qty 20)
> 3. If linear < floor → use the floor
> 4. Run 4-wave AI validation to confirm the specific floor price
>
> Do NOT linearly extrapolate $30.86/sq ft below ~0.06 sq ft.
> Do NOT use 3024592's $2.75 as a mechanical parity clone for future items — validate fresh.

---

## Step 5 — Band Positioning Summary

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | Governing Method |
|------|-------|----------------|-----------------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark) | 1.296 | $20.00 | Singles band ($/sq ft) |
| 1279000 (Micro-Format founding) | 0.097 | $3.00 | Micro-Format Band ($/sq ft × 2.0× benchmark) |
| 1278220 (Parity clone) | 0.097 | $3.00 | Parity with 1279000 |

sections.nesting:
### Laminator Pass Count

The narrow dimension (4.687") is the only dimension that fits the laminator's 13.5" maximum width capacity (8.813" clearance). The wide dimension (15") exceeds 13.5" and cannot be used across the laminator in any configuration. This makes lamination orientation **forced, not chosen** — the 15" dimension is necessarily the feed (length) direction, and 4.687" is necessarily the across-width dimension.

- 4.687" across laminator: 8.813" clearance — single pass ✓ (only viable orientation)
- 15" across laminator: does not fit — excluded

**One lamination pass**, consistent with every other single-label item on this account.

### Print Layout and Nesting

Label: 15" × 4.687". Roland SG3-300 max print width: 28". To keep the printed sheet in the same orientation the laminator requires (15" running in the feed direction), the label prints **15" across the 28" print bed** — matching the established convention for other 15"-wide singles on this account (e.g., 1230820, 1267140).

- Labels per row: 1 (15" ≤ 28", 13" clearance; 2 × 15" = 30" exceeds the 28" bed, so only 1 fits per row)
- At qty 20: 20 rows × 1 label = 20 labels. Straightforward single-orientation run, no complications.

### Material Cost Breakdown per Label

> Material cost version date: 2026-07-01. Unit costs from `governance/PRODUCTION.md` / `materials/orajet-3951-white.md` (Orajet 3951 verified 2026-04-22, $1.21/sq ft) and `materials/1mil-polyester-overlaminate.md` (1-mil polyester lam verified 2026-05-28, $0.2389/sq ft); full bleed ink standard verified 2026-06-01. **Computed fresh from these actual verified material files this session — no cost figures carried forward from the 4-wave AI validation record, which used invented estimates.**
>
> **Account-wide full bleed ink rule (PRICING_RULES.md §25):** All printed/laminated items on this account are priced assuming full bleed / full coverage ink at all times. Rate: $0.50/sq ft × full label sq ft.

| Component | Calculation | Per-Label Cost |
|-----------|-------------|----------------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.488 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft | $0.5905 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.488 sq ft × $0.2389/sq ft | $0.1166 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed standard) | 0.488 sq ft × $0.50/sq ft | $0.2440 |
| **Calculated total** | | **$0.9511** |
| Incidental buffer (conservative round-up) ¹ | judgment-applied | + $0.0089 |
| **Canonical `material_cost_per_unit`** | | **$0.96** |

¹ **Incidental buffer convention (account-wide rule per PRICING_RULES.md §25):** The calculated material total is rounded conservatively upward to account for setup scrap, registration pulls, and minor production waste. Here $0.9511 rounds up to $0.96, consistent with the account-wide convention of rounding printed/laminated material costs to the cent, upward.

sections.production:
1. File import/setup — open the Elliott-supplied production-ready DWG, send to RIP. ~5 min mechanical. NOT billable labor (see `PRICING_RULES.md` §22). File prep is $0 per Core Rule 7.
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. Full bleed / full coverage ANSI danger label artwork. 1 label per row at 15" across the print bed.
3. Laminate — 1-mil clear polyester overlaminate. **ONE pass — single fixed orientation.** The label's narrow dimension feeds across the laminator's width capacity with clearance to spare; the wide dimension does not fit that capacity in any configuration, so there is no alternate nesting orientation available for this item (unlike items where either dimension fits and orientation is chosen for efficiency). This is a normal equipment constraint the shop absorbs — it is not passed to the buyer in cost or lead time.
4. Contour cut — Roland follows registration marks from the print step.
5. Weed — remove excess material.
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, ANSI color/format compliance.
7. Package — flat, stacked.

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Estimated Production Time (batch of 20):** ~20–25 min total (5 min file import + 15–20 min print/lam/cut/weed/inspect/package) — consistent with other single-pass singles on this account.
- **Constraints:** Fixed lamination orientation (see above) — absorbed as normal process, not a buyer-facing limitation.

## Step 1 — Size-Class Routing

**Item:** 3024595 LBL-DNGR TIP, ELEC, CRUSH. 15" × 4.687" = 0.48824 sq ft (filed 0.488). Single label, 1 piece. Orajet 3951 + 1-mil polyester lam. ANSI danger/warning label (§29 account rule). 1 production pass.

**Dimensional scope routing (Decision Tree Step 1a from `categories/printed-laminated-orajet.md`):**

| Threshold | Value | Result |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| sq ft < 0.1? | 0.488 ≥ 0.1 | NOT Micro-Format Band |
| 0.1 ≤ sq ft < 0.5? | 0.488 in range | **SUB-SCOPE HANDLING** |
| sq ft ≥ 0.5? | 0.488 < 0.5 | NOT Singles Band |

**Routing: Sub-scope (0.1–0.5 sq ft) — the largest sub-scope data point on file, sitting at the boundary with the singles band (~0.5 sq ft).** This is NOT a singles-band item despite its proximity to that boundary.

---

## Step 2 — Governing Comparables

| Item | Sq Ft | Price (qty 20) | $/Sq Ft (qty 20) | Notes |
|------|-------|-----------------|-------------------|-------|
| 1230820 (Root benchmark / hard floor) | 1.296 | $20.00 | $15.43 | Singles band root — hard floor, applies at every tier per §31 |
| 1068270 / 1073950 (nearest singles-band neighbor) | 0.503 | $8.00 | $15.91 | Just past the sub-scope/singles boundary |
| **3024595 (this item)** | **0.488** | **$7.75** | **$15.88** | **Largest sub-scope data point — interpolates between 1210810 and 1068270/1073950** |
| 1210810 (nearest sub-scope comparable below) | 0.292 | $4.75 | $16.27 | Sub-scope |
| 1279130 | 0.148 | $3.10 | $20.95 | Sub-scope |
| 3020477 | 0.130 | $2.75 | $21.15 | Sub-scope |
| 1247120 | 0.122 | $2.75 | $22.54 | Sub-scope |

3024595 sits correctly between 1210810 ($16.27/sq ft at 0.292 sq ft) and the 0.503 sq ft singles-band position ($15.91/sq ft) — the tightest interpolation gap on the sub-scope gradient to date, consistent with it being the largest sub-scope item on file.

---

## Step 3 — Material Cost (Computed Fresh This Session)

**No cost data from the 4-wave AI validation record was used.** All margin figures generated across the 24 model responses were invented estimates per session instruction. Material cost below is computed from the actual verified material files in this repo (`materials/orajet-3951-white.md`, `materials/1mil-polyester-overlaminate.md`, and the §25 canonical ink rate).

| Component | Calculation | Cost |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Orajet 3951 cast vinyl | 0.488 × $1.21 | $0.5905 |
| 1-mil polyester overlaminate | 0.488 × $0.2389 | $0.1166 |
| Eco-solvent ink (full bleed) | 0.488 × $0.50 | $0.2440 |
| Calculated total | | **$0.9511** |
| Incidental buffer (account convention) | judgment-applied | + $0.0089 |
| **Canonical material cost** | | **$0.96** |

**$/sq ft at qty 20:** $7.75 / 0.488 = **$15.88/sq ft** — above the $15.43/sq ft root floor at every tier (see Step 5).

---

## Step 4 — 4-Wave AI Validation Summary (Already Complete — Recorded, Not Re-Derived)

**Validation method:** 4-wave atomic AI validation, 6 top-tier models per wave, 24 independent responses, run in Claude Chat per `governance/VALIDATION_PROMPTS.md`. Atomic session model — no model has visibility into other waves or other models' responses. Nick is sole decision-maker; the responses are evidence, not votes.

**Wave 1 (Build):** 5 of 6 models converged on $7.75/ea at qty 20 ($15.88/sq ft), interpolating between the 0.292 sq ft sub-scope point (1210810, $16.27/sq ft) and the 0.503 sq ft singles-band comparable (1068270/1073950, $15.91/sq ft). One outlier at $8.00 rejected — it inverts against the 0.292 sq ft point (a larger sub-scope label priced above a smaller one on $/sq ft would be defensible, but $8.00 at 0.488 sq ft = $16.39/sq ft exceeds 1210810's $16.27/sq ft despite 3024595 being the larger label — a gradient inversion).

**Wave 2 (Destruction):** Presented two competing deep-tier structures:
- **Option A** — flat $7.75 from qty 20 through 200+.
- **Option B** — step the price down below the $15.43/sq ft root floor at higher volumes.

**Unanimous ruling, 6/6: the $15.43/sq ft root floor (P/N 1230820) applies at every tier, at every quantity — not just the qty-20 rate.** Option B was rejected outright as precedent-damaging: it would teach the buyer the floor is negotiable at volume, undermining every future sub-scope item approaching this size. Option A (flat ladder) was confirmed as the only structurally valid outcome — the sole $0.25-compliant (§30) configuration that never breaches the floor. **This ruling is the founding event for `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 — see Notes and Warnings.**

**Wave 3 (Buyer Simulation):** Sean approves; the flat structure from qty 20–200+ does not trigger rejection, but multiple models flagged that the bare quote-language stub ("priced at $7.75 at your projected monthly volume") carries meaningful risk of triggering a one-line clarifying email asking why pricing doesn't step down at volume. Pushback threshold confirmed at $8.00–$8.25/label — $7.75 clears with room.

**Wave 4 (Final Synthesis):** 4 of 5 models voted YES send-as-shown with the stronger anchor line required; 1 model voted NO specifically because the anchor line wasn't yet included (not a pricing objection — all 5 agreed the tier table itself is fully correct). **Consensus: send the pricing as-is, using the upgraded quote language** (see Notes and Warnings — Quote Language Stub).

**Nick's decision:** Locked the tier table above prior to this session's file-write. No override — engine consensus (with the flat-tier floor ruling) accepted in full.

---

## Step 5 — Floor Doctrine Verification

The $15.43/sq ft root floor (P/N 1230820) is verified at every tier on this item:

| Tier | Price | $/Sq Ft | Above $15.43/sq ft Floor? |
|------|-------|---------|---------------------------|
| 1-9 | $10.75 | $22.03 | ✓ Yes (+42.8%) |
| 10-19 | $9.25 | $18.96 | ✓ Yes (+22.9%) |
| 20-49 | $7.75 | $15.88 | ✓ Yes (+2.9%) |
| 50-99 | $7.75 | $15.88 | ✓ Yes (+2.9%) |
| 100-199 | $7.75 | $15.88 | ✓ Yes (+2.9%) |
| 200+ | $7.75 | $15.88 | ✓ Yes (+2.9%) |

**No tier on this item breaches the floor.** The flat structure at 20-49 through 200+ is precisely what keeps every high-volume tier above the floor — this is the structural reasoning behind the Wave 2 ruling and the new §31 doctrine.

---

## Step 6 — §26 Invoice Protection Check

Since the 20-49 through 200+ tiers are flat at $7.75, there is no invoice-protection risk at those boundaries by definition (identical per-unit price, so no cliff can occur). Only the 1-9→10-19 and 10-19→20-49 boundaries carry a per-unit price change and require verification:

| 19/20 | 19 × $9.25 = $175.75 | 20 × $7.75 = $155.00 | $20.75 | §26: buyer charged $155.00 for qty 19 |

Both boundaries resolve cleanly under §26 — no inversions. No additional quote-email language required beyond the standard §26 handling already applied account-wide.

> Wave 4 found that the bare stub ("priced at $7.75 at your projected monthly volume") carries meaningful risk of a clarifying email asking why pricing doesn't step down at volume. **Use this locked language instead — do not default to the bare stub:**

> *"Label measures 15" × 4.687" (0.488 sq ft) in cast vinyl with polyester overlaminate. At this size and construction, the label reaches its cost floor at your projected monthly volume, so pricing holds flat from 20 units through your highest run quantities — priced at $7.75 each."*

---

> ## FIRST ARTICLE STATUS — UNCONFIRMED WITH SEAN
>
> First article has **not been confirmed** with Sean on this item. No first-article cost line has been invented or quoted. Confirm FA status directly with Sean before or alongside sending this quote.

---

> ## ORDER QUANTITY — UNCONFIRMED WITH SEAN
>
> Sean has not specified an order quantity for this item. The standard 6-tier ladder is quoted per account convention; no quantity-specific pricing has been assumed or invented. Confirm Sean's actual PO volume alongside sending the quote.

---

> ## LAMINATION ORIENTATION — FORCED, NOT CHOSEN
>
> Unlike most singles on this account, where either dimension can go across the laminator and orientation is a nesting-efficiency choice, 3024595 has **no orientation choice**: only the narrow dimension (4.687") fits the laminator's width capacity; the wide dimension (15") does not fit in that orientation at all. This is documented here using process-neutral language; do not expose the exact laminator width constraint by name in any buyer-facing content — frame as normal process (see Production Process and `PRICING_RULES.md` Communication Rule 6).

---

> ## ANSI DANGER LABEL — §29 ACCOUNT RULE
>
> This is an ANSI danger/warning label (tip-over / electrical / crush hazard). Per `PRICING_RULES.md` §29, all items printed on Orajet 3951 with polyester laminate on this account are ANSI by default — no separate ANSI premium or discount is applied; the sub-scope gradient pricing already reflects the account's full-bleed and complexity assumptions.

---

> ## ARTWORK PREP — $0
>
> Elliott Equipment Company provides production-ready DWG files for every item on this account, without exception. File prep, artwork preparation, design time, and pre-press labor are zero-cost, zero-time inputs on every Elliott job. See `PRICING_RULES.md` §22-24 and Core Rule 7.

---

> ## AI VALIDATION — COMPLETE (4-WAVE ATOMIC PROCESS, RUN IN CLAUDE CHAT)
>
> 4-wave, 6-model atomic AI validation (24 independent responses) is complete and locked prior to this session's file-write. Full documentation in Pricing Derivation Step 4. **Key outcome beyond the tier table itself: the Wave 2 ruling that the $15.43/sq ft root floor applies at every tier is now codified as `governance/PRICING_RULES.md` §31 — the governing doctrine for this item and all future sub-scope items approaching the singles-band boundary.**
